'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '福島県双葉郡浪江町請戸', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kusano-jinja}} [[JA:苕野神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11616108}} == Japanese content == {{特殊文字}} {{神社 |名称=苕野神社 |画像=[[File:Haiden of Kusano-jinja shrine.JPG|270px|苕野神社拝殿(震災前)]]
拝殿 |所在地={{jalink|福島県|Q71707}}{{jalink|双葉郡|Q1167153}}{{jalink|浪江町|Q1023937}}大字請戸字東向38 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|37|28|50.6|N|141|2|22.1|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=苕野神社}} |祭神={{jalink|淤加美神|Q644201|高龗神・闇龗神}}
{{jalink|五十猛神|Q10881556}}
{{jalink|オオヤツヒメ・ツマツヒメ|Q10935047|大屋津姫神・抓津姫神}} |社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|県社}} |創建={{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}の御世 |本殿={{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名=あんばさま |例祭={{jalink|8月6日|Q2787}}
{{jalink|8月7日|Q2781}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=苕野神社|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''苕野神社'''（くさのじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|福島県|Q71707}}{{jalink|双葉郡|Q1167153}}{{jalink|浪江町|Q1023937}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。 {{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は県社。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|延喜式}}{{jalink|式内社}}の{{jalink|陸奥国|Q907389}}百座のうちの一座ある。 === 祭神 === *{{jalink|淤加美神|Q644201|高龗神・闇龗神}}、{{jalink|五十猛神|Q10881556}}、{{jalink|オオヤツヒメ・ツマツヒメ|Q10935047|大屋津姫神・抓津姫神}}を祀る。 **「奥相志」によれば、神体は「長さ一尺、十歳くらいの童形坐像の御子八神が九体」という。 === 由緒 === 由緒書によれば、起源・創立年代は不明だが、社伝によれば第12代天皇である{{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}の御世に勧請され、{{jalink|元正天皇|Q235343}}({{jalink|715年|Q23528}})の御代には社殿を創建したという。{{jalink|桓武天皇|Q314846}}の時代、{{jalink|坂上田村麻呂|Q941979}}が東夷征伐の勅命をうけて進軍してきた際、苕野神社にて戦勝を祈願し、無事近隣の山を支配下に置く賊徒を平定した後に、神恩への報賽として神殿を建てたという。 苕野神社は{{jalink|標葉郡|Q11542238}}の神社の中で唯一の{{jalink|延喜式内社||式内社}}であり、古来から郡民や領主などから篤い尊崇を集めていた。{{jalink|保元|Q1193898}}年間の{{jalink|1156年|Q19653}}、請戸に館を築いて標葉郡を支配していた標葉左京大夫平隆義は、苕野神社への敬神の念がことに篤く、神社へ神領を寄進し社殿を造営するなどし、標葉氏の氏神として崇めたという。{{jalink|明応|Q1193841}}元年（{{jalink|1492年|Q6679}}）に相馬氏の所領となると、相馬氏からも尊崇を集め、社領の寄進や社殿の造営が行われた。 苕野神社は往古は請戸地区の沖にあった「苕野小島」という島に鎮座していた。その後、波浪などで島が崩壊したため、現在の鎮座地に遷座したという。また、苕野神社は{{jalink|茨城県|Q83273}}{{jalink|稲敷市|Q838675}}に鎮座する{{jalink|大杉神社|Q11435950}}（通称あんばさま）と関係が深い神社であり、毎年2月の第3日曜日には'''『安波祭』'''と呼ばれる「浜下り潮水神事」が催行されてきた。安波祭の大祭式典では、浦安の舞神楽・田植えおどりが舞われる他、神輿渡御、樽みこし海上荒波渡御、御潮水献備神事、早朝護摩祈祷などの神事が行われる。 {{jalink|2011年|Q1994}}{{jalink|3月11日|Q2398}}発生した{{jalink|東北地方太平洋沖地震|Q36204}}により、浪江町の沿岸部は{{jalink|津波|Q8070}}の被害を受けた。海のそばに鎮座する苕野神社も社殿がすべて流失、前宮司の家族も犠牲となった。その後も近隣の{{jalink|福島第一原子力発電所|Q114295|福島第一原発}}で起きた{{jalink|福島第一原子力発電所事故|Q171178|原子力事故}}の影響で2012年8月現在も警戒区域に指定されており、許可無く立ち入ることができない状態が続いている。 {{jalink|2012年|Q1990}}{{jalink|2月19日|Q2340}}には、{{jalink|岡山県|Q132936}}{{jalink|神社庁|Q11590703}}と岡山県神道青年会、{{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}神社庁の支援を受けて仮社殿が震災前の社殿鎮座地に造営された。 御神霊は{{jalink|飯舘村|Q674500}}に鎮座する、苕野神社の分霊社である{{jalink|綿津見神社|Q11607434}}から賜ったものである。宮司には前宮司の三女が就任し、現在も苕野神社へ奉斎している。{{Cite web|和書|title=東日本大震災復興支援|url=http://www.jinjahoncho.or.jp/reconstruction/000071.html|work= |publisher=神社本庁|date= |accessdate=2012-08-25}} ===祭神について=== 苕野神社の現在の祭神は高龗神・闇龗神、五十猛神、大屋津姫神・抓津姫神であるが、「奥相志」には祭神についての諸説が記されている。 *九柱の神女説 :大昔、人皇の初めの頃に奥州標葉郡苕野（現在の請戸地区）沖から磐楠船（{{jalink|クスノキ|Q158722}}でできた頑丈な船）が岸に漂着した。船の中を除くと、九柱の神女がおり、神女の放つ霊光と美しい顔は尋常ならざるものであった。苕野の浜に住んでいた阿部という強者はこれを怒り怪しんで、矛を片手に九柱の神女へと向かっていった。これに対し、村の新汐渡媛という夫婦が阿部を叱り制した。そして、夫婦は神女たちを尊崇して小さな建物を作り神居とした。朝になり神居に行くと、瑞雲が建物を覆い、薫香が匂い立っていた。これを見て信仰心を刺激された新汐渡媛夫婦に対して、神女たちは「私たちは東国に留まり、私たちを信仰する人々には国家安全・武運長久・子孫繁栄の願いを叶え、横難横死を防ぎ、とりわけ海上の逆波を鎮めて船に乗る人々の災難を取り除きましょう。苕野小島に宮居を建てて、そこに苕野の神として祀りなさい」と伝えた。夫婦は喜び、神女にいわれたとおりに小島へ神女たちを祀った。それ以来、海上で風のため船が進まなくなった時に苕野の神へ祈ると、逆波は鎮まり運行が容易になると伝わった。{{jalink|養老|Q1373061}}年間には、{{jalink|元正天皇|Q235343}}が苕野の神について耳にし、九柱の神像をつくり宮居に納めて祀った。その後、苕野小島が波の影響で崩壊したので陸地へ遷座したという。毎年7月7日には神衣を奉り、神主は顔を覆って神体が身につける衣を新しいものに取り替える神事が行われた。 *天竺もしくは震旦国王の妃説 :{{jalink|正徳 (日本)|Q1193848|正徳}}年間に記された「寺社好問誌」によれば、請戸の神社の祭神は天竺もしくは震旦国王の妃であると書かれている。王と王妃は不仲であり、王は妃を空舟に乗せて海へ流してしまった。妃を乗せた空船は漂流し、請戸の沖へと流れてきた。時に、村人の阿部氏という者が海に漁をしに出ており、漂流する空船を怪しんで、遠海へと引っ張りだしてしまった。海上で釣りをしていた村人である荒氏という者が、阿部氏により沖へと追いやられた空船が波に乗ってやってくるのを見た。荒氏はこの空船を接岸させて船内を覗いてみると女性がいたので、上陸してこの女性を養った。その後、小祠を波打ち際近くに建てて、この女性を村の守り神として祀ったという。 :昔は何という神様なのか分からなかったので、神託を得ようと協議がなされた。神託を得るため神楽を奉納したところ、苕野の神から「我を貴船明神と信仰すべし」という託宣があった。そのため苕野神社は'''貴布根神'''と称し、地名から請戸明神と呼ばれたという。神体は九体あり、一尺ほどの童形坐像の御子八神であるが、今まで一度も公開はされたことがないと書かれている。荒氏夫婦は束帯を着け船に乗り、この神像を傍らに安置した。毎年7月7日には「御召初め」という神事があり、麻単衣を新調し、神官が神像が身に着けている衣を取り替えたと記されている。 *新羅国の女神説 :元禄年間に藤橋月船という者が記した「標葉記」によれば、古老の話では、苕野の神は新羅国からやってきて請戸の小島に現れた女神であるという。この時初めて、阿部氏と荒氏はこの女神を信仰し、阿部氏は特に何もしなかったが、荒氏は小島に社を設けて女神を祀った。その後、{{jalink|保元|Q1193898}}年間にいたり、標葉氏の祖である平隆義が標葉郡を領有するようになると、請戸明神を鎮守神と定め、社領の寄付や社殿の修繕を行い代々篤く崇敬したという。 苕野神社の社家である鈴木家の話として「奥相志」に記載されているものによれば、苕野の神は元正天皇の時代である養老元年（丁巳）に小島へ現れ、養老から保元年間に至る四百三十年のあいだ請戸地区と{{jalink|請戸川|Q24860718}}の北岸の棚塩地区の鎮守神として崇敬され、近代になり貴布根社と呼ばれるようになったとされている。ある書によれば、養老年間に神女が波に浮かび現れたと書かれているという。また、新汐渡媛という夫婦が'''「栄え行く　松に契りて　万代の　蔭を請戸の　浜に住むらん」'''という歌を残したといい、「新汐渡媛」という夫婦の名は、上記の文に出てくる荒氏夫婦につけられた神号ではないかと考察がされている。相馬藩主である{{jalink|相馬昌胤|Q8514599}}も、苕野神社へ数首の和歌や詠歌を献上している。 === 境内（震災前） === *社殿（拝殿・幣殿・本殿） File:Torii gate of Kusano-jinja shrine.JPG|苕野神社鳥居 File:Haiden_of_Kusano-jinja_shrine_2.jpg|苕野神社拝殿 File:Honden of Kusano-jinja shrine.JPG|苕野神社本殿 *境内社 **祖霊殿・日吉神社（祭神：{{jalink|大山咋神|Q10935105}}）：祖霊殿の社殿は旧本殿であり、御神木が倒壊した際に被害を受けたので修復し祖霊殿として流用したという。 **山祇神社（祭神：{{jalink|オオヤマツミ|Q386563|大山祇神}}） **黄金山神社（祭神：{{jalink|金山彦神|Q4387856|金山彦神・金山姫神}}） Old {{jalink|Prefectural company}} |Foundation={{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}} |Main shrine={{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} |Also known as: Ambasama |Regular Festival={{jalink|August 6th|Q2787}}
{{jalink|August 7th|Q2781}}|Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Shinto Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kusano Shrine'' (Kusano Jinja) is a {{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}{{jalink|Futaba District|Q1167153}}{{jalink|Namie Town|Q1023937}}. {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is a prefectural company. {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|Engishiki}} One of the {{jalink|Mutsu Province|Q907389}} hundred troupes of {{jalink|Shikiuchi}}. === Enshrined God === *{{jalink|Nakamikami|Q644201|Takanikami/Yamikami}}, {{jalink|Isotakekami|Q10881556}}, {{jalink|Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Oyatsuhimekami/Sakutsuhimekami}}. **According to ``Okusōshi'', the divine objects are ``nine god-like eight deities in the form of seated child statues, each about 10 years old, about 1 shaku in length.'' === History === According to the history book, the origin and founding date are unknown, but according to the shrine's biography, it was commissioned during the reign of the 12th emperor, {{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}}, and the shrine was built during the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Gensho|Q235343}} ({{jalink|715|Q23528}}). It is said that in the era of {{jalink|Emperor Kanmu|Q314846}}, when {{jalink|Sakanoue Tamuramaro|Q941979}} marched on an imperial order to conquer Toi, he prayed for victory at Kagino Shrine, and after successfully subduing the bandits who had taken control of the nearby mountains, he built the temple as a reward for the divine kindness. Kasuno Shrine is the only {{jalink|Engishikinaisha||Shikiuchi}} among the shrines in {{jalink|Subbeha County|Q11542238}}, and has been highly respected by the people of the county and the feudal lords since ancient times. During the {{jalink|Hogen|Q1193898}} {{jalink|1156|Q19653}}, Takayoshi Shibeha Sakyo Taibu, who built a mansion in Ukedo and ruled over Shibeha District, had a particularly deep sense of reverence for Kagino-jinja Shrine, and it is said that he donated sacred land to the shrine, built a shrine, and worshiped it as the guardian deity of the Shibeha clan. When it became the domain of the Soma clan in the first year of {{jalink|Meio|Q1193841}} ({{jalink|1492|Q6679}}), it gained respect from the Soma clan, who donated the shrine territory and built a shrine.In ancient times, Kabunino Shrine was enshrined on an island called Kabunokojima, which was located off the coast of the Ukedo area. After that, the island collapsed due to waves and other factors, so it was relocated to its current location. In addition, Kagino Shrine is located in {{jalink|Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273}}{{jalink|Inashiki City|Q838675}}{{jalink|Osugi Shrine|Q114 35950}} (commonly known as Ambasama), the ``Hamagari Shiomizu Shinto Ritual'' called ``Anba Festival'' has been held on the third Sunday of February every year. At the Anba Festival's grand ceremony, Urayasu's dance kagura and the rice-planting dance are performed, as well as other rituals such as the mikoshi togyo, the barrel mikoshi sea raging wave togyo, the oshiomizu offering ritual, and the early morning goma prayer. {{jalink|2011|Q1994}}{{jalink|March 11|Q2398}}The coastal area of ​​Namie Town was damaged by the {{jalink|tsunami|Q8070}} due to the {{jalink|Tohoku Pacific Coast Earthquake|Q36204}}. The entire shrine building of Kakuno Shrine, which is located near the sea, was washed away, and the family of the former chief priest was also among the victims. Since then, as of August 2012, due to the {{jalink|Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant|Q114295|Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant}} accident that occurred at the nearby {{jalink|Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant|Q171178|nuclear accident}}, the area has been designated as a caution area as of August 2012, and no one can enter without permission.{{jalink|2012|Q1990}}{{jalink|February 19th|Q2340}}, {{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}}{{jalin With the support of k|Shrine Agency|Q11590703}}, Okayama Prefecture Shinto Youth Association, and {{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}} Shrine Agency, a temporary shrine building was constructed on the site where the shrine was enshrined before the earthquake. The sacred spirit was given to us by {{jalink|Watatsumi Shrine|Q11607434}}, which is a branch shrine of Kakuno Shrine, located in {{jalink|Iitate Village|Q674500}}. The third daughter of the previous chief priest was appointed as the chief priest, and she still resides at Kagino Shrine. {{Cite web|Japanese book|title=Great East Japan Earthquake Reconstruction Support|url=http://www.jinjahoncho.or.jp/reconstruction/000071.html|work= |publisher=Shinja Honcho|date= |accessdate=2012-08-25}} ===About the enshrined deity=== The current deities enshrined at Kasuno Shrine are Takani no kami, Yami no kami, Isotake no kami, Oyatsu Hime no Kami, and Sakutsu Hime no Kami, but various theories about the deities enshrined are recorded in ``Okusōshi.'' *The Nine Pillars of Goddess Theory:A long time ago, at the beginning of the Emperor's era, an Iwakusu ship (a sturdy ship made of {{jalink|camphor tree|Q158722}}) washed ashore from the coast of Oshu Shibebe-gun Kakuno (present-day Ukedo district). Outside of the ship, there were nine divine goddesses, and the spiritual light and beautiful faces they emitted were extraordinary. A strong man named Abe, who lived on Kabunohama Beach, became angry and suspicious of this, and with a spear in hand, he headed toward the Nine Goddess. In response, a couple from the village named Niishiotohime scolded Abe and stopped him. The couple built a small building to worship the goddesses and used it as a shrine. When I went to the shrine in the morning, the building was covered with clouds and the smell of incense filled the air. Seeing this, the Shinshiotohime couple were inspired by their faith, and the goddesses told them, ``We will remain in the eastern part of Japan, and for those who believe in us, we will grant their wishes for national security, long life in military affairs, and prosperity for their descendants, and we will prevent accidents and deaths, and above all, we will calm down the adverse waves at sea and eliminate disasters for those who board ships. Build a shrine on Kadeno Kojima, and enshrine it there as the god of Kadeno.'' The couple was overjoyed and went to the small island to enshrine the goddesses as they had been told. Since then, it has been said that if you pray to the god of Kagino when your ship is stuck at sea due to wind, the waves will calm down and the ship will be able to sail more easily. In the {{jalink|Yoro|Q1373061}} year, {{jalink|Emperor Gensho|Q235343}} heard about the gods of Kakuno, and created nine statues of the gods and enshrined them in the palace. After that, Kagino Kojima collapsed under the influence of waves, so it was relocated to land. Every year on July 7th, a Shinto ritual was held in which the priest covered his face and replaced the robe worn by the deity with a new one. *Tenjiku or King Shitan's wife theory:{{jalink|Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|Shōtoku}} According to the ``Temples and Shrines Questionnaire'' written in the 1980s, it is written that the deity enshrined at the Ukedo shrine is Tenjiku or the wife of King Shitan. The king and queen were not on good terms, and the king put her in an empty boat and set her adrift at sea. The empty ship carrying the princess drifted off the coast of Ukedo. One day, a villager named Mr. Abe was out fishing at sea, and became suspicious of an empty boat that was drifting adrift, so he pulled it out to sea. Mr. Ara, a villager who was fishing at sea, saw an empty boat that had been driven out to sea by Mr. Abe coming on the waves. Mr. Ara brought this empty ship to shore and when he looked inside, he saw a woman, so he went ashore and fed her. Afterwards, a small shrine was built near the water's edge to enshrine this woman as the village's guardian deity. :In the old days, people didn't know what the god was, so there was a discussion to get an oracle. When he offered Kagura to obtain an oracle, he received an oracle from the god of Kabino that said, ``You should worship me as Kifune Myojin.'' Therefore, Kabunino Shrine was called ``Kifunejin'', and it was also called Ukedo Myojin from the place name. There are nine deities, each of which is a one-foot seated child-shaped statue of the eight deities, but it is written that they have never been shown to the public. The Ara couple put on sokui and boarded the ship, enshrining this statue beside them. It is said that on July 7th of every year, there was a ritual called ``Goyosho'', in which new linen robes were made and the priests replaced the robes worn by the statues. *Goddess theory of Silla:According to the ``Hobyoki'' written by a person named Tsukifune Fujihashi during the Genroku period, an ancient story says that the god of Kakuno was a goddess who came from Silla and appeared on a small island in Ukedo. At this time, the Abe clan and the Ara clan worshiped this goddess for the first time, and although the Abe clan did nothing special, the Ara clan established a shrine on a small island to worship the goddess. Later, in the {{jalink|Hogen|Q1193898}} period, when Taira Takayoshi, the founder of the Shibeha clan, took possession of Shibeha District, he designated Ukedo Myojin as the guardian deity, donated shrine territory and repaired the shrine's buildings, and worshiped it fervently from generation to generation. According to the story of the Suzuki family, the chief priests of Kabunino Shrine, recorded in the Okusōshi, the deity of Kabunno appeared on a small island in the first year of Yoro (Chōmi), during the era of Emperor Gensho, and lived for 403 years from Yoro to Hogen. It is said that for ten years it was revered as the guardian deity of the Ukedo area and the Tanashio area on the north bank of the {{jalink|Ukedo River|Q24860718}}, and in modern times it came to be called Kifune Shrine. According to one book, it is written that a goddess appeared on the waves during the Yoro period. It is also said that a couple named Shinshiotohime left behind a song that goes, ``We will live on the beach of Ukedo, relying on the prosperous pine tree for generations to come.'' It has been speculated that the couple's name ``Shinshiotohime'' may be a divine name given to the Ara clan couple mentioned in the above sentence. The lord of the Soma domain, {{jalink|Masatane Soma|Q8514599}}, also presented several waka poems and poems to Kagino Shrine. === Precincts (before the earthquake) === *Shrine building (haiden, haiden, main shrine) File:Torii gate of Kusano-jinja shrine.JPG|Torii gate of Kusano-jinja shrine File:Haiden_of_Kusano-jinja_shrine_2.jpg|Haiden of Kusano-jinja shrine File:Honden of Kusano-jinja shrine.JPG|Honden of Kusano-jinja shrine *Precinct shrine **Soreiden Hiyoshi Shrine (enshrined deity: {{jalink|Oyama Kuijin|Q10935105}}): The shrine building of Soreiden was the former main shrine, which was damaged when the sacred tree collapsed, so it was repaired and used as the ancestors' shrine. ** Yamazumi Shrine (Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Oyamatsumi|Q386563|Oyamazumi}}) **Koganeyama Shrine (Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Kanayamahikokami|Q4387856|Kanayamahikokami/Kanayama Himekami}}) **Konpira Daimyojin/Nakiri Fudogu Shrine (enshrined deity: {{jalink|Oimono-sujin}}/{{jalink|Fudo {{jalink|August 7th|Q2781}}|Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Shinto Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kusano Shrine'' (Kusano Jinja) is a {{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}{{jalink|Futaba District|Q1167153}}{{jalink|Namie Town|Q1023937}}. {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is a prefectural company. {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|Engishiki}} One of the {{jalink|Mutsu Province|Q907389}} hundred troupes of {{jalink|Shikiuchi}}. === Enshrined God === *{{jalink|Nakamikami|Q644201|Takanikami/Yamikami}}, {{jalink|Isotakekami|Q10881556}}, {{jalink|Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Oyatsuhimekami/Sakutsuhimekami}}. **According to ``Okusōshi'', the divine objects are ``nine god-like eight deities in the form of seated child statues, each about 10 years old, about 1 shaku in length.'' === History === According to the history book, the origin and founding date are unknown, but according to the shrine's biography, it was commissioned during the reign of the 12th emperor, {{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}}, and the shrine was built during the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Gensho|Q235343}} ({{jalink|715|Q23528}}). It is said that in the era of {{jalink|Emperor Kanmu|Q314846}}, when {{jalink|Sakanoue Tamuramaro|Q941979}} marched on an imperial order to conquer Toi, he prayed for victory at Kagino Shrine, and after successfully subduing the bandits who had taken control of the nearby mountains, he built the temple as a reward for the divine kindness. Kasuno Shrine is the only {{jalink|Engishikinaisha||Shikiuchi}} among the shrines in {{jalink|Subbeha County|Q11542238}}, and has been highly respected by the people of the county and the feudal lords since ancient times. During the {{jalink|Hogen|Q1193898}} {{jalink|1156|Q19653}}, Takayoshi Shibeha Sakyo Taibu, who built a mansion in Ukedo and ruled over Shibeha District, had a particularly deep sense of reverence for Kagino-jinja Shrine, and it is said that he donated sacred land to the shrine, built a shrine, and worshiped it as the guardian deity of the Shibeha clan. When it became the domain of the Soma clan in the first year of {{jalink|Meio|Q1193841}} ({{jalink|1492|Q6679}}), it gained respect from the Soma clan, who donated the shrine territory and built a shrine.In ancient times, Kabunino Shrine was enshrined on an island called Kabunokojima, which was located off the coast of the Ukedo area. After that, the island collapsed due to waves and other factors, so it was relocated to its current location. In addition, Kagino Shrine is located in {{jalink|Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273}}{{jalink|Inashiki City|Q838675}}{{jalink|Osugi Shrine|Q114 35950}} (commonly known as Ambasama), the ``Hamagari Shiomizu Shinto Ritual'' called ``Anba Festival'' has been held on the third Sunday of February every year. At the Anba Festival's grand ceremony, Urayasu's dance kagura and the rice-planting dance are performed, as well as other rituals such as the mikoshi togyo, the barrel mikoshi sea raging wave togyo, the oshiomizu offering ritual, and the early morning goma prayer. {{jalink|2011|Q1994}}{{jalink|March 11|Q2398}}The coastal area of ​​Namie Town was damaged by the {{jalink|tsunami|Q8070}} due to the {{jalink|Tohoku Pacific Coast Earthquake|Q36204}}. The entire shrine building of Kakuno Shrine, which is located near the sea, was washed away, and the family of the former chief priest was also among the victims. Since then, as of August 2012, due to the {{jalink|Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant|Q114295|Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant}} accident that occurred at the nearby {{jalink|Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant|Q171178|nuclear accident}}, the area has been designated as a caution area as of August 2012, and no one can enter without permission.{{jalink|2012|Q1990}}{{jalink|February 19th|Q2340}}, {{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}}{{jalin With the support of k|Shrine Agency|Q11590703}}, Okayama Prefecture Shinto Youth Association, and {{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}} Shrine Agency, a temporary shrine building was constructed on the site where the shrine was enshrined before the earthquake. The sacred spirit was given to us by {{jalink|Watatsumi Shrine|Q11607434}}, which is a branch shrine of Kakuno Shrine, located in {{jalink|Iitate Village|Q674500}}. The third daughter of the previous chief priest was appointed as the chief priest, and she still resides at Kagino Shrine. {{Cite web|Japanese book|title=Great East Japan Earthquake Reconstruction Support|url=http://www.jinjahoncho.or.jp/reconstruction/000071.html|work= |publisher=Shinja Honcho|date= |accessdate=2012-08-25}} ===About the enshrined deity=== The current deities enshrined at Kasuno Shrine are Takani no kami, Yami no kami, Isotake no kami, Oyatsu Hime no Kami, and Sakutsu Hime no Kami, but various theories about the deities enshrined are recorded in ``Okusōshi.'' *The Nine Pillars of Goddess Theory:A long time ago, at the beginning of the Emperor's era, an Iwakusu ship (a sturdy ship made of {{jalink|camphor tree|Q158722}}) washed ashore from the coast of Oshu Shibebe-gun Kakuno (present-day Ukedo district). Outside of the ship, there were nine divine goddesses, and the spiritual light and beautiful faces they emitted were extraordinary. A strong man named Abe, who lived on Kabunohama Beach, became angry and suspicious of this, and with a spear in hand, he headed toward the Nine Goddess. In response, a couple from the village named Niishiotohime scolded Abe and stopped him. The couple built a small building to worship the goddesses and used it as a shrine. When I went to the shrine in the morning, the building was covered with clouds and the smell of incense filled the air. Seeing this, the Shinshiotohime couple were inspired by their faith, and the goddesses told them, ``We will remain in the eastern part of Japan, and for those who believe in us, we will grant their wishes for national security, long life in military affairs, and prosperity for their descendants, and we will prevent accidents and deaths, and above all, we will calm down the adverse waves at sea and eliminate disasters for those who board ships. Build a shrine on Kadeno Kojima, and enshrine it there as the god of Kadeno.'' The couple was overjoyed and went to the small island to enshrine the goddesses as they had been told. Since then, it has been said that if you pray to the god of Kagino when your ship is stuck at sea due to wind, the waves will calm down and the ship will be able to sail more easily. In the {{jalink|Yoro|Q1373061}} year, {{jalink|Emperor Gensho|Q235343}} heard about the gods of Kakuno, and created nine statues of the gods and enshrined them in the palace. After that, Kagino Kojima collapsed under the influence of waves, so it was relocated to land. Every year on July 7th, a Shinto ritual was held in which the priest covered his face and replaced the robe worn by the deity with a new one. *Tenjiku or King Shitan's wife theory:{{jalink|Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|Shōtoku}} According to the ``Temples and Shrines Questionnaire'' written in the 1980s, it is written that the deity enshrined at the Ukedo shrine is Tenjiku or the wife of King Shitan. The king and queen were not on good terms, and the king put her in an empty boat and set her adrift at sea. The empty ship carrying the princess drifted off the coast of Ukedo. One day, a villager named Mr. Abe was out fishing at sea, and became suspicious of an empty boat that was drifting adrift, so he pulled it out to sea. Mr. Ara, a villager who was fishing at sea, saw an empty boat that had been driven out to sea by Mr. Abe coming on the waves. Mr. Ara brought this empty ship to shore and when he looked inside, he saw a woman, so he went ashore and fed her. Afterwards, a small shrine was built near the water's edge to enshrine this woman as the village's guardian deity. :In the old days, people didn't know what the god was, so there was a discussion to get an oracle. When he offered Kagura to obtain an oracle, he received an oracle from the god of Kabino that said, ``You should worship me as Kifune Myojin.'' Therefore, Kabunino Shrine was called ``Kifunejin'', and it was also called Ukedo Myojin from the place name. There are nine deities, each of which is a one-foot seated child-shaped statue of the eight deities, but it is written that they have never been shown to the public. The Ara couple put on sokui and boarded the ship, enshrining this statue beside them. It is said that on July 7th of every year, there was a ritual called ``Goyosho'', in which new linen robes were made and the priests replaced the robes worn by the statues. *Goddess theory of Silla:According to the ``Hobyoki'' written by a person named Tsukifune Fujihashi during the Genroku period, an ancient story says that the god of Kakuno was a goddess who came from Silla and appeared on a small island in Ukedo. At this time, the Abe clan and the Ara clan worshiped this goddess for the first time, and although the Abe clan did nothing special, the Ara clan established a shrine on a small island to worship the goddess. Later, in the {{jalink|Hogen|Q1193898}} period, when Taira Takayoshi, the founder of the Shibeha clan, took possession of Shibeha District, he designated Ukedo Myojin as the guardian deity, donated shrine territory and repaired the shrine's buildings, and worshiped it fervently from generation to generation. According to the story of the Suzuki family, the chief priests of Kabunino Shrine, recorded in the Okusōshi, the deity of Kabunno appeared on a small island in the first year of Yoro (Chōmi), during the era of Emperor Gensho, and lived for 403 years from Yoro to Hogen. It is said that for ten years it was revered as the guardian deity of the Ukedo area and the Tanashio area on the north bank of the {{jalink|Ukedo River|Q24860718}}, and in modern times it came to be called Kifune Shrine. According to one book, it is written that a goddess appeared on the waves during the Yoro period. It is also said that a couple named Shinshiotohime left behind a song that goes, ``We will live on the beach of Ukedo, relying on the prosperous pine tree for generations to come.'' It has been speculated that the couple's name ``Shinshiotohime'' may be a divine name given to the Ara clan couple mentioned in the above sentence. The lord of the Soma domain, {{jalink|Masatane Soma|Q8514599}}, also presented several waka poems and poems to Kagino Shrine. === Precincts (before the earthquake) === *Shrine building (haiden, haiden, main shrine) File:Torii gate of Kusano-jinja shrine.JPG|Torii gate of Kusano-jinja shrine File:Haiden_of_Kusano-jinja_shrine_2.jpg|Haiden of Kusano-jinja shrine File:Honden of Kusano-jinja shrine.JPG|Honden of Kusano-jinja shrine *Precinct shrine **Soreiden Hiyoshi Shrine (enshrined deity: {{jalink|Oyama Kuijin|Q10935105}}): The shrine building of Soreiden was the former main shrine, which was damaged when the sacred tree collapsed, so it was repaired and used as the ancestors' shrine. ** Yamazumi Shrine (Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Oyamatsumi|Q386563|Oyamazumi}}) **Koganeyama Shrine (Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Kanayamahikokami|Q4387856|Kanayamahikokami/Kanayama Himekami}}) **Konpira Daimyojin/Nakiri Fudogu Shrine (enshrined deity: {{jalink|Oimono-sujin}}/{{jalink|Fudo Old {{jalink|Prefectural company}} |Foundation={{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}} |Main shrine={{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} |Also known as: Ambasama |Regular Festival={{jalink|August 6th|Q2787}}
{{jalink|August 7th|Q2781}}|Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Shinto Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kusano Shrine'' (Kusano Jinja) is a {{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}{{jalink|Futaba District|Q1167153}}{{jalink|Namie Town|Q1023937}}. {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is a prefectural company. {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|Engishiki}} One of the {{jalink|Mutsu Province|Q907389}} hundred troupes of {{jalink|Shikiuchi}}. === Enshrined God === *{{jalink|Nakamikami|Q644201|Takanikami/Yamikami}}, {{jalink|Isotakekami|Q10881556}}, {{jalink|Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Oyatsuhimekami/Sakutsuhimekami}}. **According to ``Okusōshi'', the divine objects are ``nine god-like eight deities in the form of seated child statues, each about 10 years old, about 1 shaku in length.'' === History === According to the history book, the origin and founding date are unknown, but according to the shrine's biography, it was commissioned during the reign of the 12th emperor, {{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}}, and the shrine was built during the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Gensho|Q235343}} ({{jalink|715|Q23528}}). It is said that in the era of {{jalink|Emperor Kanmu|Q314846}}, when {{jalink|Sakanoue Tamuramaro|Q941979}} marched on an imperial order to conquer Toi, he prayed for victory at Kagino Shrine, and after successfully subduing the bandits who had taken control of the nearby mountains, he built the temple as a reward for the divine kindness. Kasuno Shrine is the only {{jalink|Engishikinaisha||Shikiuchi}} among the shrines in {{jalink|Subbeha County|Q11542238}}, and has been highly respected by the people of the county and the feudal lords since ancient times. During the {{jalink|Hogen|Q1193898}} {{jalink|1156|Q19653}}, Takayoshi Shibeha Sakyo Taibu, who built a mansion in Ukedo and ruled over Shibeha District, had a particularly deep sense of reverence for Kagino-jinja Shrine, and it is said that he donated sacred land to the shrine, built a shrine, and worshiped it as the guardian deity of the Shibeha clan. When it became the domain of the Soma clan in the first year of {{jalink|Meio|Q1193841}} ({{jalink|1492|Q6679}}), it gained respect from the Soma clan, who donated the shrine territory and built a shrine.In ancient times, Kabunino Shrine was enshrined on an island called Kabunokojima, which was located off the coast of the Ukedo area. After that, the island collapsed due to waves and other factors, so it was relocated to its current location. In addition, Kagino Shrine is located in {{jalink|Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273}}{{jalink|Inashiki City|Q838675}}{{jalink|Osugi Shrine|Q114 35950}} (commonly known as Ambasama), the ``Hamagari Shiomizu Shinto Ritual'' called ``Anba Festival'' has been held on the third Sunday of February every year. At the Anba Festival's grand ceremony, Urayasu's dance kagura and the rice-planting dance are performed, as well as other rituals such as the mikoshi togyo, the barrel mikoshi sea raging wave togyo, the oshiomizu offering ritual, and the early morning goma prayer. {{jalink|2011|Q1994}}{{jalink|March 11|Q2398}}The coastal area of ​​Namie Town was damaged by the {{jalink|tsunami|Q8070}} due to the {{jalink|Tohoku Pacific Coast Earthquake|Q36204}}. The entire shrine building of Kakuno Shrine, which is located near the sea, was washed away, and the family of the former chief priest was also among the victims. Since then, as of August 2012, due to the {{jalink|Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant|Q114295|Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant}} accident that occurred at the nearby {{jalink|Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant|Q171178|nuclear accident}}, the area has been designated as a caution area as of August 2012, and no one can enter without permission.{{jalink|2012|Q1990}}{{jalink|February 19th|Q2340}}, {{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}}{{jalin With the support of k|Shrine Agency|Q11590703}}, Okayama Prefecture Shinto Youth Association, and {{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}} Shrine Agency, a temporary shrine building was constructed on the site where the shrine was enshrined before the earthquake. The sacred spirit was given to us by {{jalink|Watatsumi Shrine|Q11607434}}, which is a branch shrine of Kakuno Shrine, located in {{jalink|Iitate Village|Q674500}}. The third daughter of the previous chief priest was appointed as the chief priest, and she still resides at Kagino Shrine. {{Cite web|Japanese book|title=Great East Japan Earthquake Reconstruction Support|url=http://www.jinjahoncho.or.jp/reconstruction/000071.html|work= |publisher=Shinja Honcho|date= |accessdate=2012-08-25}} ===About the enshrined deity=== The current deities enshrined at Kasuno Shrine are Takani no kami, Yami no kami, Isotake no kami, Oyatsu Hime no Kami, and Sakutsu Hime no Kami, but various theories about the deities enshrined are recorded in ``Okusōshi.'' *The Nine Pillars of Goddess Theory:A long time ago, at the beginning of the Emperor's era, an Iwakusu ship (a sturdy ship made of {{jalink|camphor tree|Q158722}}) washed ashore from the coast of Oshu Shibebe-gun Kakuno (present-day Ukedo district). Outside of the ship, there were nine divine goddesses, and the spiritual light and beautiful faces they emitted were extraordinary. A strong man named Abe, who lived on Kabunohama Beach, became angry and suspicious of this, and with a spear in hand, he headed toward the Nine Goddess. In response, a couple from the village named Niishiotohime scolded Abe and stopped him. The couple built a small building to worship the goddesses and used it as a shrine. When I went to the shrine in the morning, the building was covered with clouds and the smell of incense filled the air. Seeing this, the Shinshiotohime couple were inspired by their faith, and the goddesses told them, ``We will remain in the eastern part of Japan, and for those who believe in us, we will grant their wishes for national security, long life in military affairs, and prosperity for their descendants, and we will prevent accidents and deaths, and above all, we will calm down the adverse waves at sea and eliminate disasters for those who board ships. Build a shrine on Kadeno Kojima, and enshrine it there as the god of Kadeno.'' The couple was overjoyed and went to the small island to enshrine the goddesses as they had been told. Since then, it has been said that if you pray to the god of Kagino when your ship is stuck at sea due to wind, the waves will calm down and the ship will be able to sail more easily. In the {{jalink|Yoro|Q1373061}} year, {{jalink|Emperor Gensho|Q235343}} heard about the gods of Kakuno, and created nine statues of the gods and enshrined them in the palace. After that, Kagino Kojima collapsed under the influence of waves, so it was relocated to land. Every year on July 7th, a Shinto ritual was held in which the priest covered his face and replaced the robe worn by the deity with a new one. *Tenjiku or King Shitan's wife theory:{{jalink|Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|Shōtoku}} According to the ``Temples and Shrines Questionnaire'' written in the 1980s, it is written that the deity enshrined at the Ukedo shrine is Tenjiku or the wife of King Shitan. The king and queen were not on good terms, and the king put her in an empty boat and set her adrift at sea. The empty ship carrying the princess drifted off the coast of Ukedo. One day, a villager named Mr. Abe was out fishing at sea, and became suspicious of an empty boat that was drifting adrift, so he pulled it out to sea. Mr. Ara, a villager who was fishing at sea, saw an empty boat that had been driven out to sea by Mr. Abe coming on the waves. Mr. Ara brought this empty ship to shore and when he looked inside, he saw a woman, so he went ashore and fed her. Afterwards, a small shrine was built near the water's edge to enshrine this woman as the village's guardian deity. :In the old days, people didn't know what the god was, so there was a discussion to get an oracle. When he offered Kagura to obtain an oracle, he received an oracle from the god of Kabino that said, ``You should worship me as Kifune Myojin.'' Therefore, Kabunino Shrine was called ``Kifunejin'', and it was also called Ukedo Myojin from the place name. There are nine deities, each of which is a one-foot seated child-shaped statue of the eight deities, but it is written that they have never been shown to the public. The Ara couple put on sokui and boarded the ship, enshrining this statue beside them. It is said that on July 7th of every year, there was a ritual called ``Goyosho'', in which new linen robes were made and the priests replaced the robes worn by the statues. *Goddess theory of Silla:According to the ``Hobyoki'' written by a person named Tsukifune Fujihashi during the Genroku period, an ancient story says that the god of Kakuno was a goddess who came from Silla and appeared on a small island in Ukedo. At this time, the Abe clan and the Ara clan worshiped this goddess for the first time, and although the Abe clan did nothing special, the Ara clan established a shrine on a small island to worship the goddess. Later, in the {{jalink|Hogen|Q1193898}} period, when Taira Takayoshi, the founder of the Shibeha clan, took possession of Shibeha District, he designated Ukedo Myojin as the guardian deity, donated shrine territory and repaired the shrine's buildings, and worshiped it fervently from generation to generation. According to the story of the Suzuki family, the chief priests of Kabunino Shrine, recorded in the Okusōshi, the deity of Kabunno appeared on a small island in the first year of Yoro (Chōmi), during the era of Emperor Gensho, and lived for 403 years from Yoro to Hogen. It is said that for ten years it was revered as the guardian deity of the Ukedo area and the Tanashio area on the north bank of the {{jalink|Ukedo River|Q24860718}}, and in modern times it came to be called Kifune Shrine. According to one book, it is written that a goddess appeared on the waves during the Yoro period. It is also said that a couple named Shinshiotohime left behind a song that goes, ``We will live on the beach of Ukedo, relying on the prosperous pine tree for generations to come.'' It has been speculated that the couple's name ``Shinshiotohime'' may be a divine name given to the Ara clan couple mentioned in the above sentence. The lord of the Soma domain, {{jalink|Masatane Soma|Q8514599}}, also presented several waka poems and poems to Kagino Shrine. === Precincts (before the earthquake) === *Shrine building (haiden, Old {{jalink|Prefectural company}} |Foundation={{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}} |Main shrine={{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} |Also known as: Ambasama |Regular Festival={{jalink|August 6th|Q2787}}
{{jalink|August 7th|Q2781}}|Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Shinto Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kusano Shrine'' (Kusano Jinja) is a {{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}{{jalink|Futaba District|Q1167153}}{{jalink|Namie Town|Q1023937}}. {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is a prefectural company. {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|Engishiki}} One of the {{jalink|Mutsu Province|Q907389}} hundred troupes of {{jalink|Shikiuchi}}. === Enshrined God === *{{jalink|Nakamikami|Q644201|Takanikami/Yamikami}}, {{jalink|Isotakekami|Q10881556}}, {{jalink|Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Oyatsuhimekami/Sakutsuhimekami}}. **According to ``Okusōshi'', the divine objects are ``nine god-like eight deities in the form of seated child statues, each about 10 years old, about 1 shaku in length.'' === History === According to the history book, the origin and founding date are unknown, but according to the shrine's biography, it was commissioned during the reign of the 12th emperor, {{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}}, and the shrine was built during the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Gensho|Q235343}} ({{jalink|715|Q23528}}). It is said that in the era of {{jalink|Emperor Kanmu|Q314846}}, when {{jalink|Sakanoue Tamuramaro|Q941979}} marched on an imperial order to conquer Toi, he prayed for victory at Kagino Shrine, and after successfully subduing the bandits who had taken control of the nearby mountains, he built the temple as a reward for the divine kindness. Kasuno Shrine is the only {{jalink|Engishikinaisha||Shikiuchi}} among the shrines in {{jalink|Subbeha County|Q11542238}}, and has been highly respected by the people of the county and the feudal lords since ancient times. During the {{jalink|Hogen|Q1193898}} {{jalink|1156|Q19653}}, Takayoshi Shibeha Sakyo Taibu, who built a mansion in Ukedo and ruled over Shibeha District, had a particularly deep sense of reverence for Kagino-jinja Shrine, and it is said that he donated sacred land to the shrine, built a shrine, and worshiped it as the guardian deity of the Shibeha clan. When it became the domain of the Soma clan in the first year of {{jalink|Meio|Q1193841}} ({{jalink|1492|Q6679}}), it gained respect from the Soma clan, who donated the shrine territory and built a shrine.In ancient times, Kabunino Shrine was enshrined on an island called Kabunokojima, which was located off the coast of the Ukedo area. After that, the island collapsed due to waves and other factors, so it was relocated to its current location. In addition, Kagino Shrine is located in {{jalink|Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273}}{{jalink|Inashiki City|Q838675}}{{jalink|Osugi Shrine|Q114 35950}} (commonly known as Ambasama), the ``Hamagari Shiomizu Shinto Ritual'' called ``Anba Festival'' has been held on the third Sunday of February every year. At the Anba Festival's grand ceremony, Urayasu's dance kagura and the rice-planting dance are performed, as well as other rituals such as the mikoshi togyo, the barrel mikoshi sea raging wave togyo, the oshiomizu offering ritual, and the early morning goma prayer. {{jalink|2011|Q1994}}{{jalink|March 11|Q2398}}The coastal area of ​​Namie Town was damaged by the {{jalink|tsunami|Q8070}} due to the {{jalink|Tohoku Pacific Coast Earthquake|Q36204}}. The entire shrine building of Kakuno Shrine, which is located near the sea, was washed away, and the family of the former chief priest was also among the victims. Since then, as of August 2012, due to the {{jalink|Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant|Q114295|Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant}} accident that occurred at the nearby {{jalink|Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant|Q171178|nuclear accident}}, the area has been designated as a caution area as of August 2012, and no one can enter without permission.{{jalink|2012|Q1990}}{{jalink|February 19th|Q2340}}, {{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}}{{jalin With the support of k|Shrine Agency|Q11590703}}, Okayama Prefecture Shinto Youth Association, and {{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}} Shrine Agency, a temporary shrine building was constructed on the site where the shrine was enshrined before the earthquake. The sacred spirit was given to us by {{jalink|Watatsumi Shrine|Q11607434}}, which is a branch shrine of Kakuno Shrine, located in {{jalink|Iitate Village|Q674500}}. The third daughter of the previous chief priest was appointed as the chief priest, and she still resides at Kagino Shrine. {{Cite web|Japanese book|title=Great East Japan Earthquake Reconstruction Support|url=http://www.jinjahoncho.or.jp/reconstruction/000071.html|work= |publisher=Shinja Honcho|date= |accessdate=2012-08-25}} ===About the enshrined deity=== The current deities enshrined at Kasuno Shrine are Takani no kami, Yami no kami, Isotake no kami, Oyatsu Hime no Kami, and Sakutsu Hime no Kami, but various theories about the deities enshrined are recorded in ``Okusōshi.'' *The Nine Pillars of Goddess Theory:A long time ago, at the beginning of the Emperor's era, an Iwakusu ship (a sturdy ship made of {{jalink|camphor tree|Q158722}}) washed ashore from the coast of Oshu Shibebe-gun Kakuno (present-day Ukedo district). Outside of the ship, there were nine divine goddesses, and the spiritual light and beautiful faces they emitted were extraordinary. A strong man named Abe, who lived on Kabunohama Beach, became angry and suspicious of this, and with a spear in hand, he headed toward the Nine Goddess. In response, a couple from the village named Niishiotohime scolded Abe and stopped him. The couple built a small building to worship the goddesses and used it as a shrine. When I went to the shrine in the morning, the building was covered with clouds and the smell of incense filled the air. Seeing this, the Shinshiotohime couple were inspired by their faith, and the goddesses told them, ``We will remain in the eastern part of Japan, and for those who believe in us, we will grant their wishes for national security, long life in military affairs, and prosperity for their descendants, and we will prevent accidents and deaths, and above all, we will calm down the adverse waves at sea and eliminate disasters for those who board ships. Build a shrine on Kadeno Kojima, and enshrine it there as the god of Kadeno.'' The couple was overjoyed and went to the small island to enshrine the goddesses as they had been told. Since then, it has been said that if you pray to the god of Kagino when your ship is stuck at sea due to wind, the waves will calm down and the ship will be able to sail more easily. In the {{jalink|Yoro|Q1373061}} year, {{jalink|Emperor Gensho|Q235343}} heard about the gods of Kakuno, and created nine statues of the gods and enshrined them in the palace. After that, Kagino Kojima collapsed under the influence of waves, so it was relocated to land. Every year on July 7th, a Shinto ritual was held in which the priest covered his face and replaced the robe worn by the deity with a new one. *Tenjiku or King Shitan's wife theory:{{jalink|Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|Shōtoku}} According to the ``Temples and Shrines Questionnaire'' written in the 1980s, it is written that the deity enshrined at the Ukedo shrine is Tenjiku or the wife of King Shitan. The king and queen were not on good terms, and the king put her in an empty boat and set her adrift at sea. The empty ship carrying the princess drifted off the coast of Ukedo. One day, a villager named Mr. Abe was out fishing at sea, and became suspicious of an empty boat that was drifting adrift, so he pulled it out to sea. Mr. Ara, a villager who was fishing at sea, saw an empty boat that had been driven out to sea by Mr. Abe coming on the waves. Mr. Ara brought this empty ship to shore and when he looked inside, he saw a woman, so he went ashore and fed her. Afterwards, a small shrine was built near the water's edge to enshrine this woman as the village's guardian deity. :In the old days, people didn't know what the god was, so there was a discussion to get an oracle. When he offered Kagura to obtain an oracle, he received an oracle from the god of Kabino that said, ``You should worship me as Kifune Myojin.'' Therefore, Kabunino Shrine was called ``Kifunejin'', and it was also called Ukedo Myojin from the place name. There are nine deities, each of which is a one-foot seated child-shaped statue of the eight deities, but it is written that they have never been shown to the public. The Ara couple put on sokui and boarded the ship, enshrining this statue beside them. It is said that on July 7th of every year, there was a ritual called ``Goyosho'', in which new linen robes were made and the priests replaced the robes worn by the statues. *Goddess theory of Silla:According to the ``Hobyoki'' written by a person named Tsukifune Fujihashi during the Genroku period, an ancient story says that the god of Kakuno was a goddess who came from Silla and appeared on a small island in Ukedo. At this time, the Abe clan and the Ara clan worshiped this goddess for the first time, and although the Abe clan did nothing special, the Ara clan established a shrine on a small island to worship the goddess. Later, in the {{jalink|Hogen|Q1193898}} period, when Taira Takayoshi, the founder of the Shibeha clan, took possession of Shibeha District, he designated Ukedo Myojin as the guardian deity, donated shrine territory and repaired the shrine's buildings, and worshiped it fervently from generation to generation. According to the story of the Suzuki family, the chief priests of Kabunino Shrine, recorded in the Okusōshi, the deity of Kabunno appeared on a small island in the first year of Yoro (Chōmi), during the era of Emperor Gensho, and lived for 403 years from Yoro to Hogen. It is said that for ten years it was revered as the guardian deity of the Ukedo area and the Tanashio area on the north bank of the {{jalink|Ukedo River|Q24860718}}, and in modern times it came to be called Kifune Shrine. According to one book, it is written that a goddess appeared on the waves during the Yoro period. It is also said that a couple named Shinshiotohime left behind a song that goes, ``We will live on the beach of Ukedo, relying on the prosperous pine tree for generations to come.'' It has been speculated that the couple's name ``Shinshiotohime'' may be a divine name given to the Ara clan couple mentioned in the above sentence. The lord of the Soma domain, {{jalink|Masatane Soma|Q8514599}}, also presented several waka poems and poems to Kagino Shrine. === Precincts (before the earthquake) === *Shrine building (haiden, Old {{jalink|Prefectural company}} |Foundation={{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}} |Main shrine={{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} |Also known as: Ambasama |Regular Festival={{jalink|August 6th|Q2787}}
{{jalink|August 7th|Q2781}}|Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Shinto Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kusano Shrine'' (Kusano Jinja) is a {{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}{{jalink|Futaba District|Q1167153}}{{jalink|Namie Town|Q1023937}}. {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is a prefectural company. {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|Engishiki}} One of the {{jalink|Mutsu Province|Q907389}} hundred troupes of {{jalink|Shikiuchi}}. === Enshrined God === *{{jalink|Nakamikami|Q644201|Takanikami/Yamikami}}, {{jalink|Isotakekami|Q10881556}}, {{jalink|Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Oyatsuhimekami/Sakutsuhimekami}}. **According to ``Okusōshi'', the divine objects are ``nine god-like eight deities in the form of seated child statues, each about 10 years old, about 1 shaku in length.'' === History === According to the history book, the origin and founding date are unknown, but according to the shrine's biography, it was commissioned during the reign of the 12th emperor, {{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}}, and the shrine was built during the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Gensho|Q235343}} ({{jalink|715|Q23528}}). It is said that in the era of {{jalink|Emperor Kanmu|Q314846}}, when {{jalink|Sakanoue Tamuramaro|Q941979}} marched on an imperial order to conquer Toi, he prayed for victory at Kagino Shrine, and after successfully subduing the bandits who had taken control of the nearby mountains, he built the temple as a reward for the divine kindness. Kasuno Shrine is the only {{jalink|Engishikinaisha||Shikiuchi}} among the shrines in {{jalink|Subbeha County|Q11542238}}, and has been highly respected by the people of the county and the feudal lords since ancient times. During the {{jalink|Hogen|Q1193898}} {{jalink|1156|Q19653}}, Takayoshi Shibeha Sakyo Taibu, who built a mansion in Ukedo and ruled over Shibeha District, had a particularly deep sense of reverence for Kagino-jinja Shrine, and it is said that he donated sacred land to the shrine, built a shrine, and worshiped it as the guardian deity of the Shibeha clan. When it became the domain of the Soma clan in the first year of {{jalink|Meio|Q1193841}} ({{jalink|1492|Q6679}}), it gained respect from the Soma clan, who donated the shrine territory and built a shrine.In ancient times, Kabunino Shrine was enshrined on an island called Kabunokojima, which was located off the coast of the Ukedo area. After that, the island collapsed due to waves and other factors, so it was relocated to its current location. In addition, Kagino Shrine is located in {{jalink|Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273}}{{jalink|Inashiki City|Q838675}}{{jalink|Osugi Shrine|Q114 35950}} (commonly known as Ambasama), the ``Hamagari Shiomizu Shinto Ritual'' called ``Anba Festival'' has been held on the third Sunday of February every year. At the Anba Festival's grand ceremony, Urayasu's dance kagura and the rice-planting dance are performed, as well as other rituals such as the mikoshi togyo, the barrel mikoshi sea raging wave togyo, the oshiomizu offering ritual, and the early morning goma prayer. {{jalink|2011|Q1994}}{{jalink|March 11|Q2398}}The coastal area of ​​Namie Town was damaged by the {{jalink|tsunami|Q8070}} due to the {{jalink|Tohoku Pacific Coast Earthquake|Q36204}}. The entire shrine building of Kakuno Shrine, which is located near the sea, was washed away, and the family of the former chief priest was also among the victims. Since then, as of August 2012, due to the {{jalink|Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant|Q114295|Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant}} accident that occurred at the nearby {{jalink|Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant|Q171178|nuclear accident}}, the area has been designated as a caution area as of August 2012, and no one can enter without permission.{{jalink|2012|Q1990}}{{jalink|February 19th|Q2340}}, {{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}}{{jalin With the support of k|Shrine Agency|Q11590703}}, Okayama Prefecture Shinto Youth Association, and {{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}} Shrine Agency, a temporary shrine building was constructed on the site where the shrine was enshrined before the earthquake. The sacred spirit was given to us by {{jalink|Watatsumi Shrine|Q11607434}}, which is a branch shrine of Kakuno Shrine, located in {{jalink|Iitate Village|Q674500}}. The third daughter of the previous chief priest was appointed as the chief priest, and she still resides at Kagino Shrine. {{Cite web|Japanese book|title=Great East Japan Earthquake Reconstruction Support|url=http://www.jinjahoncho.or.jp/reconstruction/000071.html|work= |publisher=Shinja Honcho|date= |accessdate=2012-08-25}} ===About the enshrined deity=== The current deities enshrined at Kasuno Shrine are Takani no kami, Yami no kami, Isotake no kami, Oyatsu Hime no Kami, and Sakutsu Hime no Kami, but various theories about the deities enshrined are recorded in ``Okusōshi.'' *The Nine Pillars of Goddess Theory:A long time ago, at the beginning of the Emperor's era, an Iwakusu ship (a sturdy ship made of {{jalink|camphor tree|Q158722}}) washed ashore from the coast of Oshu Shibebe-gun Kakuno (present-day Ukedo district). Outside of the ship, there were nine divine goddesses, and the spiritual light and beautiful faces they emitted were extraordinary. A strong man named Abe, who lived on Kabunohama Beach, became angry and suspicious of this, and with a spear in hand, he headed toward the Nine Goddess. In response, a couple from the village named Niishiotohime scolded Abe and stopped him. The couple built a small building to worship the goddesses and used it as a shrine. When I went to the shrine in the morning, the building was covered with clouds and the smell of incense filled the air. Seeing this, the Shinshiotohime couple were inspired by their faith, and the goddesses told them, ``We will remain in the eastern part of Japan, and for those who believe in us, we will grant their wishes for national security, long life in military affairs, and prosperity for their descendants, and we will prevent accidents and deaths, and above all, we will calm down the adverse waves at sea and eliminate disasters for those who board ships. Build a shrine on Kadeno Kojima, and enshrine it there as the god of Kadeno.'' The couple was overjoyed and went to the small island to enshrine the goddesses as they had been told. Since then, it has been said that if you pray to the god of Kagino when your ship is stuck at sea due to wind, the waves will calm down and the ship will be able to sail more easily. In the {{jalink|Yoro|Q1373061}} year, {{jalink|Emperor Gensho|Q235343}} heard about the gods of Kakuno, and created nine statues of the gods and enshrined them in the palace. After that, Kagino Kojima collapsed under the influence of waves, so it was relocated to land. Every year on July 7th, a Shinto ritual was held in which the priest covered his face and replaced the robe worn by the deity with a new one. *Tenjiku or King Shitan's wife theory:{{jalink|Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|Shōtoku}} According to the ``Temples and Shrines Questionnaire'' written in the 1980s, it is written that the deity enshrined at the Ukedo shrine is Tenjiku or the wife of King Shitan. The king and queen were not on good terms, and the king put her in an empty boat and set her adrift at sea. The empty ship carrying the princess drifted off the coast of Ukedo. One day, a villager named Mr. Abe was out fishing at sea, and became suspicious of an empty boat that was drifting adrift, so he pulled it out to sea. Mr. Ara, a villager who was fishing at sea, saw an empty boat that had been driven out to sea by Mr. Abe coming on the waves. Mr. Ara brought this empty ship to shore and when he looked inside, he saw a woman, so he went ashore and fed her. Afterwards, a small shrine was built near the water's edge to enshrine this woman as the village's guardian deity. :In the old days, people didn't know what the god was, so there was a discussion to get an oracle. When he offered Kagura to obtain an oracle, he received an oracle from the god of Kabino that said, ``You should worship me as Kifune Myojin.'' Therefore, Kabunino Shrine was called ``Kifunejin'', and it was also called Ukedo Myojin from the place name. There are nine deities, each of which is a one-foot seated child-shaped statue of the eight deities, but it is written that they have never been shown to the public. The Ara couple put on sokui and boarded the ship, enshrining this statue beside them. It is said that on July 7th of every year, there was a ritual called ``Goyosho'', in which new linen robes were made and the priests replaced the robes worn by the statues. *Goddess theory of Silla:According to the ``Hobyoki'' written by a person named Tsukifune Fujihashi during the Genroku period, an ancient story says that the god of Kakuno was a goddess who came from Silla and appeared on a small island in Ukedo. At this time, the Abe clan and the Ara clan worshiped this goddess for the first time, and although the Abe clan did nothing special, the Ara clan established a shrine on a small island to worship the goddess. Later, in the {{jalink|Hogen|Q1193898}} period, when Taira Takayoshi, the founder of the Shibeha clan, took possession of Shibeha District, he designated Ukedo Myojin as the guardian deity, donated shrine territory and repaired the shrine's buildings, and worshiped it fervently from generation to generation. According to the story of the Suzuki family, the chief priests of Kabunino Shrine, recorded in the Okusōshi, the deity of Kabunno appeared on a small island in the first year of Yoro (Chōmi), during the era of Emperor Gensho, and lived for 403 years from Yoro to Hogen. It is said that for ten years it was revered as the guardian deity of the Ukedo area and the Tanashio area on the north bank of the {{jalink|Ukedo River|Q24860718}}, and in modern times it came to be called Kifune Shrine. According to one book, it is written that a goddess appeared on the waves during the Yoro period. It is also said that a couple named Shinshiotohime left behind a song that goes, ``We will live on the beach of Ukedo, relying on the prosperous pine tree for generations to come.'' It has been speculated that the couple's name ``Shinshiotohime'' may be a divine name given to the Ara clan couple mentioned in the above sentence. The lord of the Soma domain, {{jalink|Masatane Soma|Q8514599}}, also presented several waka poems and poems to Kagino Shrine. === Precincts (before the earthquake) === *Shrine building (haiden, Old {{ill||lt=Prefectural company|UNKNOWN}} |Foundation={{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} |Main shrine={{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |Also known as: Ambasama |Regular Festival={{ill|lt=August 6th|Q2787|WD=Q2787}}
{{ill|lt=August 7th|Q2781|WD=Q2781}}|Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Shinto Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kusano Shrine'' (Kusano Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707}}{{ill|lt=Futaba District|Q1167153|WD=Q1167153}}{{ill|lt=Namie Town|Q1023937|WD=Q1023937}}. {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is a prefectural company. {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki}} One of the {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389}} hundred troupes of {{ill||lt=Shikiuchi|UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined God === *{{ill|lt=Nakamikami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takanikami/Yamikami}}, {{ill|lt=Isotakekami|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556}}, {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Oyatsuhimekami/Sakutsuhimekami}}. **According to ``Okusōshi'', the divine objects are ``nine god-like eight deities in the form of seated child statues, each about 10 years old, about 1 shaku in length.'' === History === According to the history book, the origin and founding date are unknown, but according to the shrine's biography, it was commissioned during the reign of the 12th emperor, {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723}}, and the shrine was built during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Gensho|Q235343|WD=Q235343}} ({{ill|lt=715|Q23528|WD=Q23528}}). It is said that in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kanmu|Q314846|WD=Q314846}}, when {{ill|lt=Sakanoue Tamuramaro|Q941979|WD=Q941979}} marched on an imperial order to conquer Toi, he prayed for victory at Kagino Shrine, and after successfully subduing the bandits who had taken control of the nearby mountains, he built the temple as a reward for the divine kindness. Kasuno Shrine is the only {{ill|lt=Engishikinaisha||WD=|lt=Shikiuchi}} among the shrines in {{ill|lt=Subbeha County|Q11542238|WD=Q11542238}}, and has been highly respected by the people of the county and the feudal lords since ancient times. During the {{ill|lt=Hogen|Q1193898|WD=Q1193898}} {{ill|lt=1156|Q19653|WD=Q19653}}, Takayoshi Shibeha Sakyo Taibu, who built a mansion in Ukedo and ruled over Shibeha District, had a particularly deep sense of reverence for Kagino-jinja Shrine, and it is said that he donated sacred land to the shrine, built a shrine, and worshiped it as the guardian deity of the Shibeha clan. When it became the domain of the Soma clan in the first year of {{ill|lt=Meio|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841}} ({{ill|lt=1492|Q6679|WD=Q6679}}), it gained respect from the Soma clan, who donated the shrine territory and built a shrine.In ancient times, Kabunino Shrine was enshrined on an island called Kabunokojima, which was located off the coast of the Ukedo area. After that, the island collapsed due to waves and other factors, so it was relocated to its current location. In addition, Kagino Shrine is located in {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273}}{{ill|lt=Inashiki City|Q838675|WD=Q838675}}{{ill||lt=Osugi Shrine|UNKNOWN}} (commonly known as Ambasama), the ``Hamagari Shiomizu Shinto Ritual'' called ``Anba Festival'' has been held on the third Sunday of February every year. At the Anba Festival's grand ceremony, Urayasu's dance kagura and the rice-planting dance are performed, as well as other rituals such as the mikoshi togyo, the barrel mikoshi sea raging wave togyo, the oshiomizu offering ritual, and the early morning goma prayer. {{ill|lt=2011|Q1994|WD=Q1994}}{{ill|lt=March 11|Q2398|WD=Q2398}}The coastal area of ​​Namie Town was damaged by the {{ill|lt=tsunami|Q8070|WD=Q8070}} due to the {{ill|lt=Tohoku Pacific Coast Earthquake|Q36204|WD=Q36204}}. The entire shrine building of Kakuno Shrine, which is located near the sea, was washed away, and the family of the former chief priest was also among the victims. Since then, as of August 2012, due to the {{ill|lt=Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant|Q114295|WD=Q114295|lt=Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant}} accident that occurred at the nearby {{ill|lt=Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant|Q171178|WD=Q171178|lt=nuclear accident}}, the area has been designated as a caution area as of August 2012, and no one can enter without permission.{{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|WD=Q1990}}{{ill|lt=February 19th|Q2340|WD=Q2340}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}{{jalin With the support of k|Shrine Agency|Q11590703}}, Okayama Prefecture Shinto Youth Association, and {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}} Shrine Agency, a temporary shrine building was constructed on the site where the shrine was enshrined before the earthquake. The sacred spirit was given to us by {{ill|lt=Watatsumi Shrine|Q11607434|WD=Q11607434}}, which is a branch shrine of Kakuno Shrine, located in {{ill|lt=Iitate Village|Q674500|WD=Q674500}}. The third daughter of the previous chief priest was appointed as the chief priest, and she still resides at Kagino Shrine. {{Cite web|Japanese book|title=Great East Japan Earthquake Reconstruction Support|url=http://www.jinjahoncho.or.jp/reconstruction/000071.html|work= |publisher=Shinja Honcho|date= |accessdate=2012-08-25}} ===About the enshrined deity=== The current deities enshrined at Kasuno Shrine are Takani no kami, Yami no kami, Isotake no kami, Oyatsu Hime no Kami, and Sakutsu Hime no Kami, but various theories about the deities enshrined are recorded in ``Okusōshi.'' *The Nine Pillars of Goddess Theory:A long time ago, at the beginning of the Emperor's era, an Iwakusu ship (a sturdy ship made of {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722}}) washed ashore from the coast of Oshu Shibebe-gun Kakuno (present-day Ukedo district). Outside of the ship, there were nine divine goddesses, and the spiritual light and beautiful faces they emitted were extraordinary. A strong man named Abe, who lived on Kabunohama Beach, became angry and suspicious of this, and with a spear in hand, he headed toward the Nine Goddess. In response, a couple from the village named Niishiotohime scolded Abe and stopped him. The couple built a small building to worship the goddesses and used it as a shrine. When I went to the shrine in the morning, the building was covered with clouds and the smell of incense filled the air. Seeing this, the Shinshiotohime couple were inspired by their faith, and the goddesses told them, ``We will remain in the eastern part of Japan, and for those who believe in us, we will grant their wishes for national security, long life in military affairs, and prosperity for their descendants, and we will prevent accidents and deaths, and above all, we will calm down the adverse waves at sea and eliminate disasters for those who board ships. Build a shrine on Kadeno Kojima, and enshrine it there as the god of Kadeno.'' The couple was overjoyed and went to the small island to enshrine the goddesses as they had been told. Since then, it has been said that if you pray to the god of Kagino when your ship is stuck at sea due to wind, the waves will calm down and the ship will be able to sail more easily. In the {{ill|lt=Yoro|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061}} year, {{ill|lt=Emperor Gensho|Q235343|WD=Q235343}} heard about the gods of Kakuno, and created nine statues of the gods and enshrined them in the palace. After that, Kagino Kojima collapsed under the influence of waves, so it was relocated to land. Every year on July 7th, a Shinto ritual was held in which the priest covered his face and replaced the robe worn by the deity with a new one. *Tenjiku or King Shitan's wife theory:{{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku}} According to the ``Temples and Shrines Questionnaire'' written in the 1980s, it is written that the deity enshrined at the Ukedo shrine is Tenjiku or the wife of King Shitan. The king and queen were not on good terms, and the king put her in an empty boat and set her adrift at sea. The empty ship carrying the princess drifted off the coast of Ukedo. One day, a villager named Mr. Abe was out fishing at sea, and became suspicious of an empty boat that was drifting adrift, so he pulled it out to sea. Mr. Ara, a villager who was fishing at sea, saw an empty boat that had been driven out to sea by Mr. Abe coming on the waves. Mr. Ara brought this empty ship to shore and when he looked inside, he saw a woman, so he went ashore and fed her. Afterwards, a small shrine was built near the water's edge to enshrine this woman as the village's guardian deity. :In the old days, people didn't know what the god was, so there was a discussion to get an oracle. When he offered Kagura to obtain an oracle, he received an oracle from the god of Kabino that said, ``You should worship me as Kifune Myojin.'' Therefore, Kabunino Shrine was called ``Kifunejin'', and it was also called Ukedo Myojin from the place name. There are nine deities, each of which is a one-foot seated child-shaped statue of the eight deities, but it is written that they have never been shown to the public. The Ara couple put on sokui and boarded the ship, enshrining this statue beside them. It is said that on July 7th of every year, there was a ritual called ``Goyosho'', in which new linen robes were made and the priests replaced the robes worn by the statues. *Goddess theory of Silla:According to the ``Hobyoki'' written by a person named Tsukifune Fujihashi during the Genroku period, an ancient story says that the god of Kakuno was a goddess who came from Silla and appeared on a small island in Ukedo. At this time, the Abe clan and the Ara clan worshiped this goddess for the first time, and although the Abe clan did nothing special, the Ara clan established a shrine on a small island to worship the goddess. Later, in the {{ill|lt=Hogen|Q1193898|WD=Q1193898}} period, when Taira Takayoshi, the founder of the Shibeha clan, took possession of Shibeha District, he designated Ukedo Myojin as the guardian deity, donated shrine territory and repaired the shrine's buildings, and worshiped it fervently from generation to generation. According to the story of the Suzuki family, the chief priests of Kabunino Shrine, recorded in the Okusōshi, the deity of Kabunno appeared on a small island in the first year of Yoro (Chōmi), during the era of Emperor Gensho, and lived for 403 years from Yoro to Hogen. It is said that for ten years it was revered as the guardian deity of the Ukedo area and the Tanashio area on the north bank of the {{ill|lt=Ukedo River|Q24860718|WD=Q24860718}}, and in modern times it came to be called Kifune Shrine. According to one book, it is written that a goddess appeared on the waves during the Yoro period. It is also said that a couple named Shinshiotohime left behind a song that goes, ``We will live on the beach of Ukedo, relying on the prosperous pine tree for generations to come.'' It has been speculated that the couple's name ``Shinshiotohime'' may be a divine name given to the Ara clan couple mentioned in the above sentence. The lord of the Soma domain, {{ill|lt=Masatane Soma|Q8514599|WD=Q8514599}}, also presented several waka poems and poems to Kagino Shrine. === Precincts (before the earthquake) === *Shrine building
