{{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Oyatsuhime/Sakutsuhime|1=Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime}} |Company rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Old {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha}} |Foundation={{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} |Main shrine={{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Also known as: Ambasama |Regular Festival={{ill|lt=August 6th|Q2787|WD=Q2787|1=August 6}}
{{ill|lt=August 7th|Q2781|WD=Q2781|1=August 7}}|Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Shinto Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kusano Shrine'' (Kusano Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Futaba District|Q1167153|WD=Q1167153|1=Futaba District, Fukushima}}{{ill|lt=Namie Town|Q1023937|WD=Q1023937|1=Namie, Fukushima}}. {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is a prefectural company. {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} One of the {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}} hundred troupes of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}. === Enshrined God === *{{ill|lt=Nakamikami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takanikami/Yamikami|1=Kuraokami}}, {{ill|lt=Isotakekami|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Oyatsuhimekami/Sakutsuhimekami|1=Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime}}. **According to ``Okusōshi'', the divine objects are ``nine god-like eight deities in the form of seated child statues, each about 10 years old, about 1 shaku in length.'' === History === According to the history book, the origin and founding date are unknown, but according to the shrine's biography, it was commissioned during the reign of the 12th emperor, {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}, and the shrine was built during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Gensho|Q235343|WD=Q235343|1=Empress Genshō}} ({{ill|lt=715|Q23528|WD=Q23528|1=715}}). It is said that in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kanmu|Q314846|WD=Q314846|1=Emperor Kanmu}}, when {{ill|lt=Sakanoue Tamuramaro|Q941979|WD=Q941979|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} marched on an imperial order to conquer Toi, he prayed for victory at Kagino Shrine, and after successfully subduing the bandits who had taken control of the nearby mountains, he built the temple as a reward for the divine kindness. Kasuno Shrine is the only {{ill|lt=Engishikinaisha|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi|1=Shikinaisha}} among the shrines in {{ill|lt=Subbeha County|Q11542238|WD=Q11542238|1=Shineha district}}, and has been highly respected by the people of the county and the feudal lords since ancient times. During the {{ill|lt=Hogen|Q1193898|WD=Q1193898|1=Hōgen (era)}} {{ill|lt=1156|Q19653|WD=Q19653|1=1156}}, Takayoshi Shibeha Sakyo Taibu, who built a mansion in Ukedo and ruled over Shibeha District, had a particularly deep sense of reverence for Kagino-jinja Shrine, and it is said that he donated sacred land to the shrine, built a shrine, and worshiped it as the guardian deity of the Shibeha clan. When it became the domain of the Soma clan in the first year of {{ill|lt=Meio|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} ({{ill|lt=1492|Q6679|WD=Q6679|1=1492}}), it gained respect from the Soma clan, who donated the shrine territory and built a shrine.In ancient times, Kabunino Shrine was enshrined on an island called Kabunokojima, which was located off the coast of the Ukedo area. After that, the island collapsed due to waves and other factors, so it was relocated to its current location. In addition, Kagino Shrine is located in {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Inashiki City|Q838675|WD=Q838675|1=Inashiki}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Osugi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (commonly known as Ambasama), the ``Hamagari Shiomizu Shinto Ritual'' called ``Anba Festival'' has been held on the third Sunday of February every year. At the Anba Festival's grand ceremony, Urayasu's dance kagura and the rice-planting dance are performed, as well as other rituals such as the mikoshi togyo, the barrel mikoshi sea raging wave togyo, the oshiomizu offering ritual, and the early morning goma prayer. {{ill|lt=2011|Q1994|WD=Q1994|1=2011}}{{ill|lt=March 11|Q2398|WD=Q2398|1=March 11}}The coastal area of ​​Namie Town was damaged by the {{ill|lt=tsunami|Q8070|WD=Q8070|1=Tsunami}} due to the {{ill|lt=Tohoku Pacific Coast Earthquake|Q36204|WD=Q36204|1=2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}}. The entire shrine building of Kakuno Shrine, which is located near the sea, was washed away, and the family of the former chief priest was also among the victims. Since then, as of August 2012, due to the {{ill|lt=Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant|Q114295|WD=Q114295|lt=Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant|1=Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant}} accident that occurred at the nearby {{ill|lt=Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant|Q171178|WD=Q171178|lt=nuclear accident|1=Fukushima nuclear accident}}, the area has been designated as a caution area as of August 2012, and no one can enter without permission.{{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|WD=Q1990|1=2012}}{{ill|lt=February 19th|Q2340|WD=Q2340|1=February 19}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{jalin With the support of k|Shrine Agency|Q11590703}}, Okayama Prefecture Shinto Youth Association, and {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}} Shrine Agency, a temporary shrine building was constructed on the site where the shrine was enshrined before the earthquake. The sacred spirit was given to us by {{ill|lt=Watatsumi Shrine|Q11607434|WD=Q11607434|1=Watatsumi Shrine}}, which is a branch shrine of Kakuno Shrine, located in {{ill|lt=Iitate Village|Q674500|WD=Q674500|1=Iitate, Fukushima}}. The third daughter of the previous chief priest was appointed as the chief priest, and she still resides at Kagino Shrine. {{Cite web|Japanese book|title=Great East Japan Earthquake Reconstruction Support|url=http://www.jinjahoncho.or.jp/reconstruction/000071.html|work= |publisher=Shinja Honcho|date= |accessdate=2012-08-25}} ===About the enshrined deity=== The current deities enshrined at Kasuno Shrine are Takani no kami, Yami no kami, Isotake no kami, Oyatsu Hime no Kami, and Sakutsu Hime no Kami, but various theories about the deities enshrined are recorded in ``Okusōshi.'' *The Nine Pillars of Goddess Theory:A long time ago, at the beginning of the Emperor's era, an Iwakusu ship (a sturdy ship made of {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}}) washed ashore from the coast of Oshu Shibebe-gun Kakuno (present-day Ukedo district). Outside of the ship, there were nine divine goddesses, and the spiritual light and beautiful faces they emitted were extraordinary. A strong man named Abe, who lived on Kabunohama Beach, became angry and suspicious of this, and with a spear in hand, he headed toward the Nine Goddess. In response, a couple from the village named Niishiotohime scolded Abe and stopped him. The couple built a small building to worship the goddesses and used it as a shrine. When I went to the shrine in the morning, the building was covered with clouds and the smell of incense filled the air. Seeing this, the Shinshiotohime couple were inspired by their faith, and the goddesses told them, ``We will remain in the eastern part of Japan, and for those who believe in us, we will grant their wishes for national security, long life in military affairs, and prosperity for their descendants, and we will prevent accidents and deaths, and above all, we will calm down the adverse waves at sea and eliminate disasters for those who board ships. Build a shrine on Kadeno Kojima, and enshrine it there as the god of Kadeno.'' The couple was overjoyed and went to the small island to enshrine the goddesses as they had been told. Since then, it has been said that if you pray to the god of Kagino when your ship is stuck at sea due to wind, the waves will calm down and the ship will be able to sail more easily. In the {{ill|lt=Yoro|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} year, {{ill|lt=Emperor Gensho|Q235343|WD=Q235343|1=Empress Genshō}} heard about the gods of Kakuno, and created nine statues of the gods and enshrined them in the palace. After that, Kagino Kojima collapsed under the influence of waves, so it was relocated to land. Every year on July 7th, a Shinto ritual was held in which the priest covered his face and replaced the robe worn by the deity with a new one. *Tenjiku or King Shitan's wife theory:{{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} According to the ``Temples and Shrines Questionnaire'' written in the 1980s, it is written that the deity enshrined at the Ukedo shrine is Tenjiku or the wife of King Shitan. The king and queen were not on good terms, and the king put her in an empty boat and set her adrift at sea. The empty ship carrying the princess drifted off the coast of Ukedo. One day, a villager named Mr. Abe was out fishing at sea, and became suspicious of an empty boat that was drifting adrift, so he pulled it out to sea. Mr. Ara, a villager who was fishing at sea, saw an empty boat that had been driven out to sea by Mr. Abe coming on the waves. Mr. Ara brought this empty ship to shore and when he looked inside, he saw a woman, so he went ashore and fed her. Afterwards, a small shrine was built near the water's edge to enshrine this woman as the village's guardian deity. :In the old days, people didn't know what the god was, so there was a discussion to get an oracle. When he offered Kagura to obtain an oracle, he received an oracle from the god of Kabino that said, ``You should worship me as Kifune Myojin.'' Therefore, Kabunino Shrine was called ``Kifunejin'', and it was also called Ukedo Myojin from the place name. There are nine deities, each of which is a one-foot seated child-shaped statue of the eight deities, but it is written that they have never been shown to the public. The Ara couple put on sokui and boarded the ship, enshrining this statue beside them. It is said that on July 7th of every year, there was a ritual called ``Goyosho'', in which new linen robes were made and the priests replaced the robes worn by the statues. *Goddess theory of Silla:According to the ``Hobyoki'' written by a person named Tsukifune Fujihashi during the Genroku period, an ancient story says that the god of Kakuno was a goddess who came from Silla and appeared on a small island in Ukedo. At this time, the Abe clan and the Ara clan worshiped this goddess for the first time, and although the Abe clan did nothing special, the Ara clan established a shrine on a small island to worship the goddess. Later, in the {{ill|lt=Hogen|Q1193898|WD=Q1193898|1=Hōgen (era)}} period, when Taira Takayoshi, the founder of the Shibeha clan, took possession of Shibeha District, he designated Ukedo Myojin as the guardian deity, donated shrine territory and repaired the shrine's buildings, and worshiped it fervently from generation to generation. According to the story of the Suzuki family, the chief priests of Kabunino Shrine, recorded in the Okusōshi, the deity of Kabunno appeared on a small island in the first year of Yoro (Chōmi), during the era of Emperor Gensho, and lived for 403 years from Yoro to Hogen. It is said that for ten years it was revered as the guardian deity of the Ukedo area and the Tanashio area on the north bank of the {{ill|lt=Ukedo River|Q24860718|WD=Q24860718|1=Ukedo river}}, and in modern times it came to be called Kifune Shrine. According to one book, it is written that a goddess appeared on the waves during the Yoro period. It is also said that a couple named Shinshiotohime left behind a song that goes, ``We will live on the beach of Ukedo, relying on the prosperous pine tree for generations to come.'' It has been speculated that the couple's name ``Shinshiotohime'' may be a divine name given to the Ara clan couple mentioned in the above sentence. The lord of the Soma domain, {{ill|lt=Masatane Soma|Q8514599|WD=Q8514599|1=Sōma Masatane}}, also presented several waka poems and poems to Kagino Shrine. === Precincts (before the earthquake) === {{ill|lt=August 7th|Q2781|WD=Q2781|1=August 7}}|Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Shinto Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kusano Shrine'' (Kusano Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Futaba District|Q1167153|WD=Q1167153|1=Futaba District, Fukushima}}{{ill|lt=Namie Town|Q1023937|WD=Q1023937|1=Namie, Fukushima}}. {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is a prefectural company. {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} One of the {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}} hundred troupes of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}. === Enshrined God === *{{ill|lt=Nakamikami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takanikami/Yamikami|1=Kuraokami}}, {{ill|lt=Isotakekami|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Oyatsuhimekami/Sakutsuhimekami|1=Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime}}. **According to ``Okusōshi'', the divine objects are ``nine god-like eight deities in the form of seated child statues, each about 10 years old, about 1 shaku in length.'' === History === According to the history book, the origin and founding date are unknown, but according to the shrine's biography, it was commissioned during the reign of the 12th emperor, {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}, and the shrine was built during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Gensho|Q235343|WD=Q235343|1=Empress Genshō}} ({{ill|lt=715|Q23528|WD=Q23528|1=715}}). It is said that in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kanmu|Q314846|WD=Q314846|1=Emperor Kanmu}}, when {{ill|lt=Sakanoue Tamuramaro|Q941979|WD=Q941979|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} marched on an imperial order to conquer Toi, he prayed for victory at Kagino Shrine, and after successfully subduing the bandits who had taken control of the nearby mountains, he built the temple as a reward for the divine kindness. Kasuno Shrine is the only {{ill|lt=Engishikinaisha|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi|1=Shikinaisha}} among the shrines in {{ill|lt=Subbeha County|Q11542238|WD=Q11542238|1=Shineha district}}, and has been highly respected by the people of the county and the feudal lords since ancient times. During the {{ill|lt=Hogen|Q1193898|WD=Q1193898|1=Hōgen (era)}} {{ill|lt=1156|Q19653|WD=Q19653|1=1156}}, Takayoshi Shibeha Sakyo Taibu, who built a mansion in Ukedo and ruled over Shibeha District, had a particularly deep sense of reverence for Kagino-jinja Shrine, and it is said that he donated sacred land to the shrine, built a shrine, and worshiped it as the guardian deity of the Shibeha clan. When it became the domain of the Soma clan in the first year of {{ill|lt=Meio|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} ({{ill|lt=1492|Q6679|WD=Q6679|1=1492}}), it gained respect from the Soma clan, who donated the shrine territory and built a shrine.In ancient times, Kabunino Shrine was enshrined on an island called Kabunokojima, which was located off the coast of the Ukedo area. After that, the island collapsed due to waves and other factors, so it was relocated to its current location. In addition, Kagino Shrine is located in {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Inashiki City|Q838675|WD=Q838675|1=Inashiki}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Osugi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (commonly known as Ambasama), the ``Hamagari Shiomizu Shinto Ritual'' called ``Anba Festival'' has been held on the third Sunday of February every year. At the Anba Festival's grand ceremony, Urayasu's dance kagura and the rice-planting dance are performed, as well as other rituals such as the mikoshi togyo, the barrel mikoshi sea raging wave togyo, the oshiomizu offering ritual, and the early morning goma prayer. {{ill|lt=2011|Q1994|WD=Q1994|1=2011}}{{ill|lt=March 11|Q2398|WD=Q2398|1=March 11}}The coastal area of ​​Namie Town was damaged by the {{ill|lt=tsunami|Q8070|WD=Q8070|1=Tsunami}} due to the {{ill|lt=Tohoku Pacific Coast Earthquake|Q36204|WD=Q36204|1=2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}}. The entire shrine building of Kakuno Shrine, which is located near the sea, was washed away, and the family of the former chief priest was also among the victims. Since then, as of August 2012, due to the {{ill|lt=Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant|Q114295|WD=Q114295|lt=Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant|1=Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant}} accident that occurred at the nearby {{ill|lt=Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant|Q171178|WD=Q171178|lt=nuclear accident|1=Fukushima nuclear accident}}, the area has been designated as a caution area as of August 2012, and no one can enter without permission.{{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|WD=Q1990|1=2012}}{{ill|lt=February 19th|Q2340|WD=Q2340|1=February 19}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{jalin With the support of k|Shrine Agency|Q11590703}}, Okayama Prefecture Shinto Youth Association, and {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}} Shrine Agency, a temporary shrine building was constructed on the site where the shrine was enshrined before the earthquake. The sacred spirit was given to us by {{ill|lt=Watatsumi Shrine|Q11607434|WD=Q11607434|1=Watatsumi Shrine}}, which is a branch shrine of Kakuno Shrine, located in {{ill|lt=Iitate Village|Q674500|WD=Q674500|1=Iitate, Fukushima}}. The third daughter of the previous chief priest was appointed as the chief priest, and she still resides at Kagino Shrine. {{Cite web|Japanese book|title=Great East Japan Earthquake Reconstruction Support|url=http://www.jinjahoncho.or.jp/reconstruction/000071.html|work= |publisher=Shinja Honcho|date= |accessdate=2012-08-25}} ===About the enshrined deity=== The current deities enshrined at Kasuno Shrine are Takani no kami, Yami no kami, Isotake no kami, Oyatsu Hime no Kami, and Sakutsu Hime no Kami, but various theories about the deities enshrined are recorded in ``Okusōshi.'' *The Nine Pillars of Goddess Theory:A long time ago, at the beginning of the Emperor's era, an Iwakusu ship (a sturdy ship made of {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}}) washed ashore from the coast of Oshu Shibebe-gun Kakuno (present-day Ukedo district). Outside of the ship, there were nine divine goddesses, and the spiritual light and beautiful faces they emitted were extraordinary. A strong man named Abe, who lived on Kabunohama Beach, became angry and suspicious of this, and with a spear in hand, he headed toward the Nine Goddess. In response, a couple from the village named Niishiotohime scolded Abe and stopped him. The couple built a small building to worship the goddesses and used it as a shrine. When I went to the shrine in the morning, the building was covered with clouds and the smell of incense filled the air. Seeing this, the Shinshiotohime couple were inspired by their faith, and the goddesses told them, ``We will remain in the eastern part of Japan, and for those who believe in us, we will grant their wishes for national security, long life in military affairs, and prosperity for their descendants, and we will prevent accidents and deaths, and above all, we will calm down the adverse waves at sea and eliminate disasters for those who board ships. Build a shrine on Kadeno Kojima, and enshrine it there as the god of Kadeno.'' The couple was overjoyed and went to the small island to enshrine the goddesses as they had been told. Since then, it has been said that if you pray to the god of Kagino when your ship is stuck at sea due to wind, the waves will calm down and the ship will be able to sail more easily. In the {{ill|lt=Yoro|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} year, {{ill|lt=Emperor Gensho|Q235343|WD=Q235343|1=Empress Genshō}} heard about the gods of Kakuno, and created nine statues of the gods and enshrined them in the palace. After that, Kagino Kojima collapsed under the influence of waves, so it was relocated to land. Every year on July 7th, a Shinto ritual was held in which the priest covered his face and replaced the robe worn by the deity with a new one. *Tenjiku or King Shitan's wife theory:{{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} According to the ``Temples and Shrines Questionnaire'' written in the 1980s, it is written that the deity enshrined at the Ukedo shrine is Tenjiku or the wife of King Shitan. The king and queen were not on good terms, and the king put her in an empty boat and set her adrift at sea. The empty ship carrying the princess drifted off the coast of Ukedo. One day, a villager named Mr. Abe was out fishing at sea, and became suspicious of an empty boat that was drifting adrift, so he pulled it out to sea. Mr. Ara, a villager who was fishing at sea, saw an empty boat that had been driven out to sea by Mr. Abe coming on the waves. Mr. Ara brought this empty ship to shore and when he looked inside, he saw a woman, so he went ashore and fed her. Afterwards, a small shrine was built near the water's edge to enshrine this woman as the village's guardian deity. :In the old days, people didn't know what the god was, so there was a discussion to get an oracle. When he offered Kagura to obtain an oracle, he received an oracle from the god of Kabino that said, ``You should worship me as Kifune Myojin.'' Therefore, Kabunino Shrine was called ``Kifunejin'', and it was also called Ukedo Myojin from the place name. There are nine deities, each of which is a one-foot seated child-shaped statue of the eight deities, but it is written that they have never been shown to the public. The Ara couple put on sokui and boarded the ship, enshrining this statue beside them. It is said that on July 7th of every year, there was a ritual called ``Goyosho'', in which new linen robes were made and the priests replaced the robes worn by the statues. *Goddess theory of Silla:According to the ``Hobyoki'' written by a person named Tsukifune Fujihashi during the Genroku period, an ancient story says that the god of Kakuno was a goddess who came from Silla and appeared on a small island in Ukedo. At this time, the Abe clan and the Ara clan worshiped this goddess for the first time, and although the Abe clan did nothing special, the Ara clan established a shrine on a small island to worship the goddess. Later, in the {{ill|lt=Hogen|Q1193898|WD=Q1193898|1=Hōgen (era)}} period, when Taira Takayoshi, the founder of the Shibeha clan, took possession of Shibeha District, he designated Ukedo Myojin as the guardian deity, donated shrine territory and repaired the shrine's buildings, and worshiped it fervently from generation to generation. According to the story of the Suzuki family, the chief priests of Kabunino Shrine, recorded in the Okusōshi, the deity of Kabunno appeared on a small island in the first year of Yoro (Chōmi), during the era of Emperor Gensho, and lived for 403 years from Yoro to Hogen. It is said that for ten years it was revered as the guardian deity of the Ukedo area and the Tanashio area on the north bank of the {{ill|lt=Ukedo River|Q24860718|WD=Q24860718|1=Ukedo river}}, and in modern times it came to be called Kifune Shrine. According to one book, it is written that a goddess appeared on the waves during the Yoro period. It is also said that a couple named Shinshiotohime left behind a song that goes, ``We will live on the beach of Ukedo, relying on the prosperous pine tree for generations to come.'' It has been speculated that the couple's name ``Shinshiotohime'' may be a divine name given to the Ara clan couple mentioned in the above sentence. The lord of the Soma domain, {{ill|lt=Masatane Soma|Q8514599|WD=Q8514599|1=Sōma Masatane}}, also presented several waka poems and poems to Kagino Shrine. === Precincts (before the earthquake) === {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Oyatsuhime/Sakutsuhime|1=Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime}} |Company rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Old {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha}} |Foundation={{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} |Main shrine={{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Also known as: Ambasama |Regular Festival={{ill|lt=August 6th|Q2787|WD=Q2787|1=August 6}}
{{ill|lt=August 7th|Q2781|WD=Q2781|1=August 7}}|Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Shinto Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kusano Shrine'' (Kusano Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Futaba District|Q1167153|WD=Q1167153|1=Futaba District, Fukushima}}{{ill|lt=Namie Town|Q1023937|WD=Q1023937|1=Namie, Fukushima}}. {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is a prefectural company. {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} One of the {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}} hundred troupes of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}. === Enshrined God === *{{ill|lt=Nakamikami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takanikami/Yamikami|1=Kuraokami}}, {{ill|lt=Isotakekami|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Oyatsuhimekami/Sakutsuhimekami|1=Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime}}. **According to ``Okusōshi'', the divine objects are ``nine god-like eight deities in the form of seated child statues, each about 10 years old, about 1 shaku in length.'' === History === According to the history book, the origin and founding date are unknown, but according to the shrine's biography, it was commissioned during the reign of the 12th emperor, {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}, and the shrine was built during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Gensho|Q235343|WD=Q235343|1=Empress Genshō}} ({{ill|lt=715|Q23528|WD=Q23528|1=715}}). It is said that in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kanmu|Q314846|WD=Q314846|1=Emperor Kanmu}}, when {{ill|lt=Sakanoue Tamuramaro|Q941979|WD=Q941979|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} marched on an imperial order to conquer Toi, he prayed for victory at Kagino Shrine, and after successfully subduing the bandits who had taken control of the nearby mountains, he built the temple as a reward for the divine kindness. Kasuno Shrine is the only {{ill|lt=Engishikinaisha|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi|1=Shikinaisha}} among the shrines in {{ill|lt=Subbeha County|Q11542238|WD=Q11542238|1=Shineha district}}, and has been highly respected by the people of the county and the feudal lords since ancient times. During the {{ill|lt=Hogen|Q1193898|WD=Q1193898|1=Hōgen (era)}} {{ill|lt=1156|Q19653|WD=Q19653|1=1156}}, Takayoshi Shibeha Sakyo Taibu, who built a mansion in Ukedo and ruled over Shibeha District, had a particularly deep sense of reverence for Kagino-jinja Shrine, and it is said that he donated sacred land to the shrine, built a shrine, and worshiped it as the guardian deity of the Shibeha clan. When it became the domain of the Soma clan in the first year of {{ill|lt=Meio|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} ({{ill|lt=1492|Q6679|WD=Q6679|1=1492}}), it gained respect from the Soma clan, who donated the shrine territory and built a shrine.In ancient times, Kabunino Shrine was enshrined on an island called Kabunokojima, which was located off the coast of the Ukedo area. After that, the island collapsed due to waves and other factors, so it was relocated to its current location. In addition, Kagino Shrine is located in {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Inashiki City|Q838675|WD=Q838675|1=Inashiki}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Osugi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (commonly known as Ambasama), the ``Hamagari Shiomizu Shinto Ritual'' called ``Anba Festival'' has been held on the third Sunday of February every year. At the Anba Festival's grand ceremony, Urayasu's dance kagura and the rice-planting dance are performed, as well as other rituals such as the mikoshi togyo, the barrel mikoshi sea raging wave togyo, the oshiomizu offering ritual, and the early morning goma prayer. {{ill|lt=2011|Q1994|WD=Q1994|1=2011}}{{ill|lt=March 11|Q2398|WD=Q2398|1=March 11}}The coastal area of ​​Namie Town was damaged by the {{ill|lt=tsunami|Q8070|WD=Q8070|1=Tsunami}} due to the {{ill|lt=Tohoku Pacific Coast Earthquake|Q36204|WD=Q36204|1=2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}}. The entire shrine building of Kakuno Shrine, which is located near the sea, was washed away, and the family of the former chief priest was also among the victims. Since then, as of August 2012, due to the {{ill|lt=Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant|Q114295|WD=Q114295|lt=Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant|1=Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant}} accident that occurred at the nearby {{ill|lt=Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant|Q171178|WD=Q171178|lt=nuclear accident|1=Fukushima nuclear accident}}, the area has been designated as a caution area as of August 2012, and no one can enter without permission.{{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|WD=Q1990|1=2012}}{{ill|lt=February 19th|Q2340|WD=Q2340|1=February 19}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{jalin With the support of k|Shrine Agency|Q11590703}}, Okayama Prefecture Shinto Youth Association, and {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}} Shrine Agency, a temporary shrine building was constructed on the site where the shrine was enshrined before the earthquake. The sacred spirit was given to us by {{ill|lt=Watatsumi Shrine|Q11607434|WD=Q11607434|1=Watatsumi Shrine}}, which is a branch shrine of Kakuno Shrine, located in {{ill|lt=Iitate Village|Q674500|WD=Q674500|1=Iitate, Fukushima}}. The third daughter of the previous chief priest was appointed as the chief priest, and she still resides at Kagino Shrine. {{Cite web|Japanese book|title=Great East Japan Earthquake Reconstruction Support|url=http://www.jinjahoncho.or.jp/reconstruction/000071.html|work= |publisher=Shinja Honcho|date= |accessdate=2012-08-25}} ===About the enshrined deity=== The current deities enshrined at Kasuno Shrine are Takani no kami, Yami no kami, Isotake no kami, Oyatsu Hime no Kami, and Sakutsu Hime no Kami, but various theories about the deities enshrined are recorded in ``Okusōshi.'' *The Nine Pillars of Goddess Theory:A long time ago, at the beginning of the Emperor's era, an Iwakusu ship (a sturdy ship made of {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}}) washed ashore from the coast of Oshu Shibebe-gun Kakuno (present-day Ukedo district). Outside of the ship, there were nine divine goddesses, and the spiritual light and beautiful faces they emitted were extraordinary. A strong man named Abe, who lived on Kabunohama Beach, became angry and suspicious of this, and with a spear in hand, he headed toward the Nine Goddess. In response, a couple from the village named Niishiotohime scolded Abe and stopped him. The couple built a small building to worship the goddesses and used it as a shrine. When I went to the shrine in the morning, the building was covered with clouds and the smell of incense filled the air. Seeing this, the Shinshiotohime couple were inspired by their faith, and the goddesses told them, ``We will remain in the eastern part of Japan, and for those who believe in us, we will grant their wishes for national security, long life in military affairs, and prosperity for their descendants, and we will prevent accidents and deaths, and above all, we will calm down the adverse waves at sea and eliminate disasters for those who board ships. Build a shrine on Kadeno Kojima, and enshrine it there as the god of Kadeno.'' The couple was overjoyed and went to the small island to enshrine the goddesses as they had been told. Since then, it has been said that if you pray to the god of Kagino when your ship is stuck at sea due to wind, the waves will calm down and the ship will be able to sail more easily. In the {{ill|lt=Yoro|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} year, {{ill|lt=Emperor Gensho|Q235343|WD=Q235343|1=Empress Genshō}} heard about the gods of Kakuno, and created nine statues of the gods and enshrined them in the palace. After that, Kagino Kojima collapsed under the influence of waves, so it was relocated to land. Every year on July 7th, a Shinto ritual was held in which the priest covered his face and replaced the robe worn by the deity with a new one. *Tenjiku or King Shitan's wife theory:{{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} According to the ``Temples and Shrines Questionnaire'' written in the 1980s, it is written that the deity enshrined at the Ukedo shrine is Tenjiku or the wife of King Shitan. The king and queen were not on good terms, and the king put her in an empty boat and set her adrift at sea. The empty ship carrying the princess drifted off the coast of Ukedo. One day, a villager named Mr. Abe was out fishing at sea, and became suspicious of an empty boat that was drifting adrift, so he pulled it out to sea. Mr. Ara, a villager who was fishing at sea, saw an empty boat that had been driven out to sea by Mr. Abe coming on the waves. Mr. Ara brought this empty ship to shore and when he looked inside, he saw a woman, so he went ashore and fed her. Afterwards, a small shrine was built near the water's edge to enshrine this woman as the village's guardian deity. :In the old days, people didn't know what the god was, so there was a discussion to get an oracle. When he offered Kagura to obtain an oracle, he received an oracle from the god of Kabino that said, ``You should worship me as Kifune Myojin.'' Therefore, Kabunino Shrine was called ``Kifunejin'', and it was also called Ukedo Myojin from the place name. There are nine deities, each of which is a one-foot seated child-shaped statue of the eight deities, but it is written that they have never been shown to the public. The Ara couple put on sokui and boarded the ship, enshrining this statue beside them. It is said that on July 7th of every year, there was a ritual called ``Goyosho'', in which new linen robes were made and the priests replaced the robes worn by the statues. *Goddess theory of Silla:According to the ``Hobyoki'' written by a person named Tsukifune Fujihashi during the Genroku period, an ancient story says that the god of Kakuno was a goddess who came from Silla and appeared on a small island in Ukedo. At this time, the Abe clan and the Ara clan worshiped this goddess for the first time, and although the Abe clan did nothing special, the Ara clan established a shrine on a small island to worship the goddess. Later, in the {{ill|lt=Hogen|Q1193898|WD=Q1193898|1=Hōgen (era)}} period, when Taira Takayoshi, the founder of the Shibeha clan, took possession of Shibeha District, he designated Ukedo Myojin as the guardian deity, donated shrine territory and repaired the shrine's buildings, and worshiped it fervently from generation to generation. According to the story of the Suzuki family, the chief priests of Kabunino Shrine, recorded in the Okusōshi, the deity of Kabunno appeared on a small island in the first year of Yoro (Chōmi), during the era of Emperor Gensho, and lived for 403 years from Yoro to Hogen. It is said that for ten years it was revered as the guardian deity of the Ukedo area and the Tanashio area on the north bank of the {{ill|lt=Ukedo River|Q24860718|WD=Q24860718|1=Ukedo river}}, and in modern times it came to be called Kifune Shrine. According to one book, it is written that a goddess appeared on the waves during the Yoro period. It is also said that a couple named Shinshiotohime left behind a song that goes, ``We will live on the beach of Ukedo, relying on the prosperous pine tree for generations to come.'' It has been speculated that the couple's name ``Shinshiotohime'' may be a divine name given to the Ara clan couple mentioned in the above sentence. The lord of the Soma domain, {{ill|lt=Masatane Soma|Q8514599|WD=Q8514599|1=Sōma Masatane}}, also presented several waka poems and poems to Kagino Shrine. === Precincts (before the earthquake) === Old {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha}} |Foundation={{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|qid=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} |Main shrine={{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Also known as: Ambasama |Regular Festival={{ill|lt=August 6th|Q2787|qid=Q2787|1=August 6}}
{{ill|lt=August 7th|Q2781|qid=Q2781|1=August 7}}|Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Shinto Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kusano Shrine'' (Kusano Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|qid=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Futaba District|Q1167153|qid=Q1167153|1=Futaba District, Fukushima}}{{ill|lt=Namie Town|Q1023937|qid=Q1023937|1=Namie, Fukushima}}. {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=Old company rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is a prefectural company. {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} One of the {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|qid=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}} hundred troupes of {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}. === Enshrined God === *{{ill|lt=Nakamikami|Q644201|qid=Q644201|lt=Takanikami/Yamikami|1=Kuraokami}}, {{ill|lt=Isotakekami|Q10881556|qid=Q10881556|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|qid=Q10935047|lt=Oyatsuhimekami/Sakutsuhimekami|1=Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime}}. **According to ``Okusōshi'', the divine objects are ``nine god-like eight deities in the form of seated child statues, each about 10 years old, about 1 shaku in length.'' === History === According to the history book, the origin and founding date are unknown, but according to the shrine's biography, it was commissioned during the reign of the 12th emperor, {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|qid=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}, and the shrine was built during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Gensho|Q235343|qid=Q235343|1=Empress Genshō}} ({{ill|lt=715|Q23528|qid=Q23528|1=715}}). It is said that in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kanmu|Q314846|qid=Q314846|1=Emperor Kanmu}}, when {{ill|lt=Sakanoue Tamuramaro|Q941979|qid=Q941979|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} marched on an imperial order to conquer Toi, he prayed for victory at Kagino Shrine, and after successfully subduing the bandits who had taken control of the nearby mountains, he built the temple as a reward for the divine kindness. Kasuno Shrine is the only {{ill|lt=Engishikinaisha|UNKNOWN|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi|1=Shikinaisha}} among the shrines in {{ill|lt=Subbeha County|Q11542238|qid=Q11542238|1=Shineha district}}, and has been highly respected by the people of the county and the feudal lords since ancient times. During the {{ill|lt=Hogen|Q1193898|qid=Q1193898|1=Hōgen (era)}} {{ill|lt=1156|Q19653|qid=Q19653|1=1156}}, Takayoshi Shibeha Sakyo Taibu, who built a mansion in Ukedo and ruled over Shibeha District, had a particularly deep sense of reverence for Kagino-jinja Shrine, and it is said that he donated sacred land to the shrine, built a shrine, and worshiped it as the guardian deity of the Shibeha clan. When it became the domain of the Soma clan in the first year of {{ill|lt=Meio|Q1193841|qid=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} ({{ill|lt=1492|Q6679|qid=Q6679|1=1492}}), it gained respect from the Soma clan, who donated the shrine territory and built a shrine.In ancient times, Kabunino Shrine was enshrined on an island called Kabunokojima, which was located off the coast of the Ukedo area. After that, the island collapsed due to waves and other factors, so it was relocated to its current location. In addition, Kagino Shrine is located in {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|qid=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Inashiki City|Q838675|qid=Q838675|1=Inashiki}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Osugi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (commonly known as Ambasama), the ``Hamagari Shiomizu Shinto Ritual'' called ``Anba Festival'' has been held on the third Sunday of February every year. At the Anba Festival's grand ceremony, Urayasu's dance kagura and the rice-planting dance are performed, as well as other rituals such as the mikoshi togyo, the barrel mikoshi sea raging wave togyo, the oshiomizu offering ritual, and the early morning goma prayer. {{ill|lt=2011|Q1994|qid=Q1994|1=2011}}{{ill|lt=March 11|Q2398|qid=Q2398|1=March 11}}The coastal area of ​​Namie Town was damaged by the {{ill|lt=tsunami|Q8070|qid=Q8070|1=Tsunami}} due to the {{ill|lt=Tohoku Pacific Coast Earthquake|Q36204|qid=Q36204|1=2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}}. The entire shrine building of Kakuno Shrine, which is located near the sea, was washed away, and the family of the former chief priest was also among the victims. Since then, as of August 2012, due to the {{ill|lt=Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant|Q114295|qid=Q114295|lt=Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant|1=Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant}} accident that occurred at the nearby {{ill|lt=Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant|Q171178|qid=Q171178|lt=nuclear accident|1=Fukushima nuclear accident}}, the area has been designated as a caution area as of August 2012, and no one can enter without permission.{{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|qid=Q1990|1=2012}}{{ill|lt=February 19th|Q2340|qid=Q2340|1=February 19}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|qid=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{jalin With the support of k|Shrine Agency|Q11590703}}, Okayama Prefecture Shinto Youth Association, and {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|qid=Q1490|1=Tokyo}} Shrine Agency, a temporary shrine building was constructed on the site where the shrine was enshrined before the earthquake. The sacred spirit was given to us by {{ill|lt=Watatsumi Shrine|Q11607434|qid=Q11607434|1=Watatsumi Shrine}}, which is a branch shrine of Kakuno Shrine, located in {{ill|lt=Iitate Village|Q674500|qid=Q674500|1=Iitate, Fukushima}}. The third daughter of the previous chief priest was appointed as the chief priest, and she still resides at Kagino Shrine. {{Cite web|Japanese book|title=Great East Japan Earthquake Reconstruction Support|url=http://www.jinjahoncho.or.jp/reconstruction/000071.html|work= |publisher=Shinja Honcho|date= |accessdate=2012-08-25}} ===About the enshrined deity=== The current deities enshrined at Kasuno Shrine are Takani no kami, Yami no kami, Isotake no kami, Oyatsu Hime no Kami, and Sakutsu Hime no Kami, but various theories about the deities enshrined are recorded in ``Okusōshi.'' *The Nine Pillars of Goddess Theory:A long time ago, at the beginning of the Emperor's era, an Iwakusu ship (a sturdy ship made of {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|qid=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}}) washed ashore from the coast of Oshu Shibebe-gun Kakuno (present-day Ukedo district). Outside of the ship, there were nine divine goddesses, and the spiritual light and beautiful faces they emitted were extraordinary. A strong man named Abe, who lived on Kabunohama Beach, became angry and suspicious of this, and with a spear in hand, he headed toward the Nine Goddess. In response, a couple from the village named Niishiotohime scolded Abe and stopped him. The couple built a small building to worship the goddesses and used it as a shrine. When I went to the shrine in the morning, the building was covered with clouds and the smell of incense filled the air. Seeing this, the Shinshiotohime couple were inspired by their faith, and the goddesses told them, ``We will remain in the eastern part of Japan, and for those who believe in us, we will grant their wishes for national security, long life in military affairs, and prosperity for their descendants, and we will prevent accidents and deaths, and above all, we will calm down the adverse waves at sea and eliminate disasters for those who board ships. Build a shrine on Kadeno Kojima, and enshrine it there as the god of Kadeno.'' The couple was overjoyed and went to the small island to enshrine the goddesses as they had been told. Since then, it has been said that if you pray to the god of Kagino when your ship is stuck at sea due to wind, the waves will calm down and the ship will be able to sail more easily. In the {{ill|lt=Yoro|Q1373061|qid=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} year, {{ill|lt=Emperor Gensho|Q235343|qid=Q235343|1=Empress Genshō}} heard about the gods of Kakuno, and created nine statues of the gods and enshrined them in the palace. After that, Kagino Kojima collapsed under the influence of waves, so it was relocated to land. Every year on July 7th, a Shinto ritual was held in which the priest covered his face and replaced the robe worn by the deity with a new one. *Tenjiku or King Shitan's wife theory:{{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|qid=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} According to the ``Temples and Shrines Questionnaire'' written in the 1980s, it is written that the deity enshrined at the Ukedo shrine is Tenjiku or the wife of King Shitan. The king and queen were not on good terms, and the king put her in an empty boat and set her adrift at sea. The empty ship carrying the princess drifted off the coast of Ukedo. One day, a villager named Mr. Abe was out fishing at sea, and became suspicious of an empty boat that was drifting adrift, so he pulled it out to sea. Mr. Ara, a villager who was fishing at sea, saw an empty boat that had been driven out to sea by Mr. Abe coming on the waves. Mr. Ara brought this empty ship to shore and when he looked inside, he saw a woman, so he went ashore and fed her. Afterwards, a small shrine was built near the water's edge to enshrine this woman as the village's guardian deity. :In the old days, people didn't know what the god was, so there was a discussion to get an oracle. When he offered Kagura to obtain an oracle, he received an oracle from the god of Kabino that said, ``You should worship me as Kifune Myojin.'' Therefore, Kabunino Shrine was called ``Kifunejin'', and it was also called Ukedo Myojin from the place name. There are nine deities, each of which is a one-foot seated child-shaped statue of the eight deities, but it is written that they have never been shown to the public. The Ara couple put on sokui and boarded the ship, enshrining this statue beside them. It is said that on July 7th of every year, there was a ritual called ``Goyosho'', in which new linen robes were made and the priests replaced the robes worn by the statues. *Goddess theory of Silla:According to the ``Hobyoki'' written by a person named Tsukifune Fujihashi during the Genroku period, an ancient story says that the god of Kakuno was a goddess who came from Silla and appeared on a small island in Ukedo. At this time, the Abe clan and the Ara clan worshiped this goddess for the first time, and although the Abe clan did nothing special, the Ara clan established a shrine on a small island to worship the goddess. Later, in the {{ill|lt=Hogen|Q1193898|qid=Q1193898|1=Hōgen (era)}} period, when Taira Takayoshi, the founder of the Shibeha clan, took possession of Shibeha District, he designated Ukedo Myojin as the guardian deity, donated shrine territory and repaired the shrine's buildings, and worshiped it fervently from generation to generation. According to the story of the Suzuki family, the chief priests of Kabunino Shrine, recorded in the Okusōshi, the deity of Kabunno appeared on a small island in the first year of Yoro (Chōmi), during the era of Emperor Gensho, and lived for 403 years from Yoro to Hogen. It is said that for ten years it was revered as the guardian deity of the Ukedo area and the Tanashio area on the north bank of the {{ill|lt=Ukedo River|Q24860718|qid=Q24860718|1=Ukedo river}}, and in modern times it came to be called Kifune Shrine. According to one book, it is written that a goddess appeared on the waves during the Yoro period. It is also said that a couple named Shinshiotohime left behind a song that goes, ``We will live on the beach of Ukedo, relying on the prosperous pine tree for generations to come.'' It has been speculated that the couple's name ``Shinshiotohime'' may be a divine name given to the Ara clan couple mentioned in the above sentence. The lord of the Soma domain, {{ill|lt=Masatane Soma|Q8514599|qid=Q8514599|1=Sōma Masatane}}, also presented several waka poems and poems to Kagino Shrine. === Precincts (before the earthquake) === *Shrine building (haiden, haiden, main shrine) File:Torii gate of Kusano-jinja shrine.JPG|Torii gate of Kusano-jinja shrine File:Haiden_of_Kusano-jinja_shrine_2.jpg|Haiden of Kusano-jinja shrine File:Honden of Kusano-jinja shrine.JPG|Honden of Kusano-jinja shrine *Precinct shrine **Soreiden Hiyoshi Shrine (enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Oyama Kuijin|Q10935105|qid=Q10935105|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}): The shrine building of Soreiden was the former main shrine, which was damaged when the sacred tree collapsed, so it was repaired and used as the ancestors' shrine. ** Yamazumi Shrine (Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Oyamatsumi|Q386563|qid=Q386563|lt=Oyamazumi|1=Ōyamatsumi}}) **Koganeyama Shrine (Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Kanayamahikokami|Q4387856|qid=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahikokami/Kanayama Himekami|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}) **Konpira Daimyojin/Nakiri Fudogu Shrine (enshrined deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oimono-sujin|1=UNKNOWN}}/{{ill|lt=Fudo Myoo|Q337624|qid=Q337624|lt=Nakiri Fudo Myoo|1=Acala}}) File:Soreiden and Hie-jinja shrine.JPG|Soreiden and Hie-jinja Shrine File:Yamazumi-jinja shrine.JPG|Yamazumi Shrine File:Koganeyama-jinja shrine.JPG|Koganeyama ShrineFile:Konpira-Daimyoujin shrine and Namikiri-Fudouguu.JPG|Konpira Daimyojin/Namikiri Fudoguu === References === *Kanno Shrine Visiting Guide Kabuno Shrine Office *Oku Soshi (Soma City History 4) Old {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha}} |Foundation={{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|qid=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} |Main shrine={{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Also known as: Ambasama |Regular Festival={{ill|lt=August 6th|Q2787|qid=Q2787|1=August 6}}
{{ill|lt=August 7th|Q2781|qid=Q2781|1=August 7}}|Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Shinto Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kusano Shrine'' (Kusano Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|qid=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Futaba District|Q1167153|qid=Q1167153|1=Futaba District, Fukushima}}{{ill|lt=Namie Town|Q1023937|qid=Q1023937|1=Namie, Fukushima}}. {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=Old company rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is a prefectural company. {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} One of the {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|qid=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}} hundred troupes of {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}. === Enshrined God === *{{ill|lt=Nakamikami|Q644201|qid=Q644201|lt=Takanikami/Yamikami|1=Kuraokami}}, {{ill|lt=Isotakekami|Q10881556|qid=Q10881556|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|qid=Q10935047|lt=Oyatsuhimekami/Sakutsuhimekami|1=Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime}}. **According to ``Okusōshi'', the divine objects are ``nine god-like eight deities in the form of seated child statues, each about 10 years old, about 1 shaku in length.'' === History === According to the history book, the origin and founding date are unknown, but according to the shrine's biography, it was commissioned during the reign of the 12th emperor, {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|qid=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}, and the shrine was built during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Gensho|Q235343|qid=Q235343|1=Empress Genshō}} ({{ill|lt=715|Q23528|qid=Q23528|1=715}}). It is said that in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kanmu|Q314846|qid=Q314846|1=Emperor Kanmu}}, when {{ill|lt=Sakanoue Tamuramaro|Q941979|qid=Q941979|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} marched on an imperial order to conquer Toi, he prayed for victory at Kagino Shrine, and after successfully subduing the bandits who had taken control of the nearby mountains, he built the temple as a reward for the divine kindness. Kasuno Shrine is the only {{ill|lt=Engishikinaisha|UNKNOWN|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi|1=Shikinaisha}} among the shrines in {{ill|lt=Subbeha County|Q11542238|qid=Q11542238|1=Shineha district}}, and has been highly respected by the people of the county and the feudal lords since ancient times. During the {{ill|lt=Hogen|Q1193898|qid=Q1193898|1=Hōgen (era)}} {{ill|lt=1156|Q19653|qid=Q19653|1=1156}}, Takayoshi Shibeha Sakyo Taibu, who built a mansion in Ukedo and ruled over Shibeha District, had a particularly deep sense of reverence for Kagino-jinja Shrine, and it is said that he donated sacred land to the shrine, built a shrine, and worshiped it as the guardian deity of the Shibeha clan. When it became the domain of the Soma clan in the first year of {{ill|lt=Meio|Q1193841|qid=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} ({{ill|lt=1492|Q6679|qid=Q6679|1=1492}}), it gained respect from the Soma clan, who donated the shrine territory and built a shrine.In ancient times, Kabunino Shrine was enshrined on an island called Kabunokojima, which was located off the coast of the Ukedo area. After that, the island collapsed due to waves and other factors, so it was relocated to its current location. In addition, Kagino Shrine is located in {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|qid=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Inashiki City|Q838675|qid=Q838675|1=Inashiki}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Osugi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (commonly known as Ambasama), the ``Hamagari Shiomizu Shinto Ritual'' called ``Anba Festival'' has been held on the third Sunday of February every year. At the Anba Festival's grand ceremony, Urayasu's dance kagura and the rice-planting dance are performed, as well as other rituals such as the mikoshi togyo, the barrel mikoshi sea raging wave togyo, the oshiomizu offering ritual, and the early morning goma prayer. {{ill|lt=2011|Q1994|qid=Q1994|1=2011}}{{ill|lt=March 11|Q2398|qid=Q2398|1=March 11}}The coastal area of ​​Namie Town was damaged by the {{ill|lt=tsunami|Q8070|qid=Q8070|1=Tsunami}} due to the {{ill|lt=Tohoku Pacific Coast Earthquake|Q36204|qid=Q36204|1=2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}}. The entire shrine building of Kakuno Shrine, which is located near the sea, was washed away, and the family of the former chief priest was also among the victims. Since then, as of August 2012, due to the {{ill|lt=Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant|Q114295|qid=Q114295|lt=Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant|1=Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant}} accident that occurred at the nearby {{ill|lt=Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant|Q171178|qid=Q171178|lt=nuclear accident|1=Fukushima nuclear accident}}, the area has been designated as a caution area as of August 2012, and no one can enter without permission.{{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|qid=Q1990|1=2012}}{{ill|lt=February 19th|Q2340|qid=Q2340|1=February 19}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|qid=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{jalin With the support of k|Shrine Agency|Q11590703}}, Okayama Prefecture Shinto Youth Association, and {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|qid=Q1490|1=Tokyo}} Shrine Agency, a temporary shrine building was constructed on the site where the shrine was enshrined before the earthquake. The sacred spirit was given to us by {{ill|lt=Watatsumi Shrine|Q11607434|qid=Q11607434|1=Watatsumi Shrine}}, which is a branch shrine of Kakuno Shrine, located in {{ill|lt=Iitate Village|Q674500|qid=Q674500|1=Iitate, Fukushima}}. The third daughter of the previous chief priest was appointed as the chief priest, and she still resides at Kagino Shrine. {{Cite web|Japanese book|title=Great East Japan Earthquake Reconstruction Support|url=http://www.jinjahoncho.or.jp/reconstruction/000071.html|work= |publisher=Shinja Honcho|date= |accessdate=2012-08-25}} ===About the enshrined deity=== The current deities enshrined at Kasuno Shrine are Takani no kami, Yami no kami, Isotake no kami, Oyatsu Hime no Kami, and Sakutsu Hime no Kami, but various theories about the deities enshrined are recorded in ``Okusōshi.'' *The Nine Pillars of Goddess Theory:A long time ago, at the beginning of the Emperor's era, an Iwakusu ship (a sturdy ship made of {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|qid=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}}) washed ashore from the coast of Oshu Shibebe-gun Kakuno (present-day Ukedo district). Outside of the ship, there were nine divine goddesses, and the spiritual light and beautiful faces they emitted were extraordinary. A strong man named Abe, who lived on Kabunohama Beach, became angry and suspicious of this, and with a spear in hand, he headed toward the Nine Goddess. In response, a couple from the village named Niishiotohime scolded Abe and stopped him. The couple built a small building to worship the goddesses and used it as a shrine. When I went to the shrine in the morning, the building was covered with clouds and the smell of incense filled the air. Seeing this, the Shinshiotohime couple were inspired by their faith, and the goddesses told them, ``We will remain in the eastern part of Japan, and for those who believe in us, we will grant their wishes for national security, long life in military affairs, and prosperity for their descendants, and we will prevent accidents and deaths, and above all, we will calm down the adverse waves at sea and eliminate disasters for those who board ships. Build a shrine on Kadeno Kojima, and enshrine it there as the god of Kadeno.'' The couple was overjoyed and went to the small island to enshrine the goddesses as they had been told. Since then, it has been said that if you pray to the god of Kagino when your ship is stuck at sea due to wind, the waves will calm down and the ship will be able to sail more easily. In the {{ill|lt=Yoro|Q1373061|qid=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} year, {{ill|lt=Emperor Gensho|Q235343|qid=Q235343|1=Empress Genshō}} heard about the gods of Kakuno, and created nine statues of the gods and enshrined them in the palace. After that, Kagino Kojima collapsed under the influence of waves, so it was relocated to land. Every year on July 7th, a Shinto ritual was held in which the priest covered his face and replaced the robe worn by the deity with a new one. *Tenjiku or King Shitan's wife theory:{{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|qid=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} According to the ``Temples and Shrines Questionnaire'' written in the 1980s, it is written that the deity enshrined at the Ukedo shrine is Tenjiku or the wife of King Shitan. The king and queen were not on good terms, and the king put her in an empty boat and set her adrift at sea. The empty ship carrying the princess drifted off the coast of Ukedo. One day, a villager named Mr. Abe was out fishing at sea, and became suspicious of an empty boat that was drifting adrift, so he pulled it out to sea. Mr. Ara, a villager who was fishing at sea, saw an empty boat that had been driven out to sea by Mr. Abe coming on the waves. Mr. Ara brought this empty ship to shore and when he looked inside, he saw a woman, so he went ashore and fed her. Afterwards, a small shrine was built near the water's edge to enshrine this woman as the village's guardian deity. :In the old days, people didn't know what the god was, so there was a discussion to get an oracle. When he offered Kagura to obtain an oracle, he received an oracle from the god of Kabino that said, ``You should worship me as Kifune Myojin.'' Therefore, Kabunino Shrine was called ``Kifunejin'', and it was also called Ukedo Myojin from the place name. There are nine deities, each of which is a one-foot seated child-shaped statue of the eight deities, but it is written that they have never been shown to the public. The Ara couple put on sokui and boarded the ship, enshrining this statue beside them. It is said that on July 7th of every year, there was a ritual called ``Goyosho'', in which new linen robes were made and the priests replaced the robes worn by the statues. *Goddess theory of Silla:According to the ``Hobyoki'' written by a person named Tsukifune Fujihashi during the Genroku period, an ancient story says that the god of Kakuno was a goddess who came from Silla and appeared on a small island in Ukedo. At this time, the Abe clan and the Ara clan worshiped this goddess for the first time, and although the Abe clan did nothing special, the Ara clan established a shrine on a small island to worship the goddess. Later, in the {{ill|lt=Hogen|Q1193898|qid=Q1193898|1=Hōgen (era)}} period, when Taira Takayoshi, the founder of the Shibeha clan, took possession of Shibeha District, he designated Ukedo Myojin as the guardian deity, donated shrine territory and repaired the shrine's buildings, and worshiped it fervently from generation to generation. According to the story of the Suzuki family, the chief priests of Kabunino Shrine, recorded in the Okusōshi, the deity of Kabunno appeared on a small island in the first year of Yoro (Chōmi), during the era of Emperor Gensho, and lived for 403 years from Yoro to Hogen. It is said that for ten years it was revered as the guardian deity of the Ukedo area and the Tanashio area on the north bank of the {{ill|lt=Ukedo River|Q24860718|qid=Q24860718|1=Ukedo river}}, and in modern times it came to be called Kifune Shrine. According to one book, it is written that a goddess appeared on the waves during the Yoro period. It is also said that a couple named Shinshiotohime left behind a song that goes, ``We will live on the beach of Ukedo, relying on the prosperous pine tree for generations to come.'' It has been speculated that the couple's name ``Shinshiotohime'' may be a divine name given to the Ara clan couple mentioned in the above sentence. The lord of the Soma domain, {{ill|lt=Masatane Soma|Q8514599|qid=Q8514599|1=Sōma Masatane}}, also presented several waka poems and poems to Kagino Shrine. === Precincts (before the earthquake) === Old {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha}} |Foundation={{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|qid=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} |Main shrine={{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Also known as: Ambasama |Regular Festival={{ill|lt=August 6th|Q2787|qid=Q2787|1=August 6}}
{{ill|lt=August 7th|Q2781|qid=Q2781|1=August 7}}|Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Shinto Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kusano Shrine'' (Kusano Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|qid=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Futaba District|Q1167153|qid=Q1167153|1=Futaba District, Fukushima}}{{ill|lt=Namie Town|Q1023937|qid=Q1023937|1=Namie, Fukushima}}. {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=Old company rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is a prefectural company. {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} One of the {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|qid=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}} hundred troupes of {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}. === Enshrined God === *{{ill|lt=Nakamikami|Q644201|qid=Q644201|lt=Takanikami/Yamikami|1=Kuraokami}}, {{ill|lt=Isotakekami|Q10881556|qid=Q10881556|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|qid=Q10935047|lt=Oyatsuhimekami/Sakutsuhimekami|1=Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime}}. **According to ``Okusōshi'', the divine objects are ``nine god-like eight deities in the form of seated child statues, each about 10 years old, about 1 shaku in length.'' === History === According to the history book, the origin and founding date are unknown, but according to the shrine's biography, it was commissioned during the reign of the 12th emperor, {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|qid=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}, and the shrine was built during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Gensho|Q235343|qid=Q235343|1=Empress Genshō}} ({{ill|lt=715|Q23528|qid=Q23528|1=715}}). It is said that in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kanmu|Q314846|qid=Q314846|1=Emperor Kanmu}}, when {{ill|lt=Sakanoue Tamuramaro|Q941979|qid=Q941979|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} marched on an imperial order to conquer Toi, he prayed for victory at Kagino Shrine, and after successfully subduing the bandits who had taken control of the nearby mountains, he built the temple as a reward for the divine kindness. Kasuno Shrine is the only {{ill|lt=Engishikinaisha|UNKNOWN|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi|1=Shikinaisha}} among the shrines in {{ill|lt=Subbeha County|Q11542238|qid=Q11542238|1=Shineha district}}, and has been highly respected by the people of the county and the feudal lords since ancient times. During the {{ill|lt=Hogen|Q1193898|qid=Q1193898|1=Hōgen (era)}} {{ill|lt=1156|Q19653|qid=Q19653|1=1156}}, Takayoshi Shibeha Sakyo Taibu, who built a mansion in Ukedo and ruled over Shibeha District, had a particularly deep sense of reverence for Kagino-jinja Shrine, and it is said that he donated sacred land to the shrine, built a shrine, and worshiped it as the guardian deity of the Shibeha clan. When it became the domain of the Soma clan in the first year of {{ill|lt=Meio|Q1193841|qid=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} ({{ill|lt=1492|Q6679|qid=Q6679|1=1492}}), it gained respect from the Soma clan, who donated the shrine territory and built a shrine.In ancient times, Kabunino Shrine was enshrined on an island called Kabunokojima, which was located off the coast of the Ukedo area. After that, the island collapsed due to waves and other factors, so it was relocated to its current location. In addition, Kagino Shrine is located in {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|qid=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Inashiki City|Q838675|qid=Q838675|1=Inashiki}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Osugi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (commonly known as Ambasama), the ``Hamagari Shiomizu Shinto Ritual'' called ``Anba Festival'' has been held on the third Sunday of February every year. At the Anba Festival's grand ceremony, Urayasu's dance kagura and the rice-planting dance are performed, as well as other rituals such as the mikoshi togyo, the barrel mikoshi sea raging wave togyo, the oshiomizu offering ritual, and the early morning goma prayer. {{ill|lt=2011|Q1994|qid=Q1994|1=2011}}{{ill|lt=March 11|Q2398|qid=Q2398|1=March 11}}The coastal area of ​​Namie Town was damaged by the {{ill|lt=tsunami|Q8070|qid=Q8070|1=Tsunami}} due to the {{ill|lt=Tohoku Pacific Coast Earthquake|Q36204|qid=Q36204|1=2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}}. The entire shrine building of Kakuno Shrine, which is located near the sea, was washed away, and the family of the former chief priest was also among the victims. Since then, as of August 2012, due to the {{ill|lt=Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant|Q114295|qid=Q114295|lt=Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant|1=Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant}} accident that occurred at the nearby {{ill|lt=Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant|Q171178|qid=Q171178|lt=nuclear accident|1=Fukushima nuclear accident}}, the area has been designated as a caution area as of August 2012, and no one can enter without permission.{{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|qid=Q1990|1=2012}}{{ill|lt=February 19th|Q2340|qid=Q2340|1=February 19}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|qid=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{jalin With the support of k|Shrine Agency|Q11590703}}, Okayama Prefecture Shinto Youth Association, and {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|qid=Q1490|1=Tokyo}} Shrine Agency, a temporary shrine building was constructed on the site where the shrine was enshrined before the earthquake. The sacred spirit was given to us by {{ill|lt=Watatsumi Shrine|Q11607434|qid=Q11607434|1=Watatsumi Shrine}}, which is a branch shrine of Kakuno Shrine, located in {{ill|lt=Iitate Village|Q674500|qid=Q674500|1=Iitate, Fukushima}}. The third daughter of the previous chief priest was appointed as the chief priest, and she still resides at Kagino Shrine. {{Cite web|Japanese book|title=Great East Japan Earthquake Reconstruction Support|url=http://www.jinjahoncho.or.jp/reconstruction/000071.html|work= |publisher=Shinja Honcho|date= |accessdate=2012-08-25}} ===About the enshrined deity=== The current deities enshrined at Kasuno Shrine are Takani no kami, Yami no kami, Isotake no kami, Oyatsu Hime no Kami, and Sakutsu Hime no Kami, but various theories about the deities enshrined are recorded in ``Okusōshi.'' *The Nine Pillars of Goddess Theory:A long time ago, at the beginning of the Emperor's era, an Iwakusu ship (a sturdy ship made of {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|qid=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}}) washed ashore from the coast of Oshu Shibebe-gun Kakuno (present-day Ukedo district). Outside of the ship, there were nine divine goddesses, and the spiritual light and beautiful faces they emitted were extraordinary. A strong man named Abe, who lived on Kabunohama Beach, became angry and suspicious of this, and with a spear in hand, he headed toward the Nine Goddess. In response, a couple from the village named Niishiotohime scolded Abe and stopped him. The couple built a small building to worship the goddesses and used it as a shrine. When I went to the shrine in the morning, the building was covered with clouds and the smell of incense filled the air. Seeing this, the Shinshiotohime couple were inspired by their faith, and the goddesses told them, ``We will remain in the eastern part of Japan, and for those who believe in us, we will grant their wishes for national security, long life in military affairs, and prosperity for their descendants, and we will prevent accidents and deaths, and above all, we will calm down the adverse waves at sea and eliminate disasters for those who board ships. Build a shrine on Kadeno Kojima, and enshrine it there as the god of Kadeno.'' The couple was overjoyed and went to the small island to enshrine the goddesses as they had been told. Since then, it has been said that if you pray to the god of Kagino when your ship is stuck at sea due to wind, the waves will calm down and the ship will be able to sail more easily. In the {{ill|lt=Yoro|Q1373061|qid=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} year, {{ill|lt=Emperor Gensho|Q235343|qid=Q235343|1=Empress Genshō}} heard about the gods of Kakuno, and created nine statues of the gods and enshrined them in the palace. After that, Kagino Kojima collapsed under the influence of waves, so it was relocated to land. Every year on July 7th, a Shinto ritual was held in which the priest covered his face and replaced the robe worn by the deity with a new one. *Tenjiku or King Shitan's wife theory:{{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|qid=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} According to the ``Temples and Shrines Questionnaire'' written in the 1980s, it is written that the deity enshrined at the Ukedo shrine is Tenjiku or the wife of King Shitan. The king and queen were not on good terms, and the king put her in an empty boat and set her adrift at sea. The empty ship carrying the princess drifted off the coast of Ukedo. One day, a villager named Mr. Abe was out fishing at sea, and became suspicious of an empty boat that was drifting adrift, so he pulled it out to sea. Mr. Ara, a villager who was fishing at sea, saw an empty boat that had been driven out to sea by Mr. Abe coming on the waves. Mr. Ara brought this empty ship to shore and when he looked inside, he saw a woman, so he went ashore and fed her. Afterwards, a small shrine was built near the water's edge to enshrine this woman as the village's guardian deity. :In the old days, people didn't know what the god was, so there was a discussion to get an oracle. When he offered Kagura to obtain an oracle, he received an oracle from the god of Kabino that said, ``You should worship me as Kifune Myojin.'' Therefore, Kabunino Shrine was called ``Kifunejin'', and it was also called Ukedo Myojin from the place name. There are nine deities, each of which is a one-foot seated child-shaped statue of the eight deities, but it is written that they have never been shown to the public. The Ara couple put on sokui and boarded the ship, enshrining this statue beside them. It is said that on July 7th of every year, there was a ritual called ``Goyosho'', in which new linen robes were made and the priests replaced the robes worn by the statues. *Goddess theory of Silla:According to the ``Hobyoki'' written by a person named Tsukifune Fujihashi during the Genroku period, an ancient story says that the god of Kakuno was a goddess who came from Silla and appeared on a small island in Ukedo. At this time, the Abe clan and the Ara clan worshiped this goddess for the first time, and although the Abe clan did nothing special, the Ara clan established a shrine on a small island to worship the goddess. Later, in the {{ill|lt=Hogen|Q1193898|qid=Q1193898|1=Hōgen (era)}} period, when Taira Takayoshi, the founder of the Shibeha clan, took possession of Shibeha District, he designated Ukedo Myojin as the guardian deity, donated shrine territory and repaired the shrine's buildings, and worshiped it fervently from generation to generation. According to the story of the Suzuki family, the chief priests of Kabunino Shrine, recorded in the Okusōshi, the deity of Kabunno appeared on a small island in the first year of Yoro (Chōmi), during the era of Emperor Gensho, and lived for 403 years from Yoro to Hogen. It is said that for ten years it was revered as the guardian deity of the Ukedo area and the Tanashio area on the north bank of the {{ill|lt=Ukedo River|Q24860718|qid=Q24860718|1=Ukedo river}}, and in modern times it came to be called Kifune Shrine. According to one book, it is written that a goddess appeared on the waves during the Yoro period. It is also said that a couple named Shinshiotohime left behind a song that goes, ``We will live on the beach of Ukedo, relying on the prosperous pine tree for generations to come.'' It has been speculated that the couple's name ``Shinshiotohime'' may be a divine name given to the Ara clan couple mentioned in the above sentence. The lord of the Soma domain, {{ill|lt=Masatane Soma|Q8514599|qid=Q8514599|1=Sōma Masatane}}, also presented several waka poems and poems to Kagino Shrine. === Precincts (before the earthquake) ===
